echo "  Target: django-web-app ($APP_SG)"
echo ""
echo "Team can now:"
echo "  - SSH into Django instances"
echo "  - View application logs"
echo "  - Run database migrations"
echo "  - Deploy updates"
echo "  - Debug production issues"
Adding SSH access from team VPN... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-ssh123456789ab", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 22, "ToPort": 22, "CidrIpv4": "203.0.113.0/24", "IsEgress": false, "Tags": [] }] } ✓ SSH rule added SSH access configured successfully
# Allow SSH from team VPN from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import json ssh_access_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'ssh_port': 22, 'vpn_cidr': '203.0.113.0/24' } print(f"SSH Access Rule - Team VPN Access") print(f"App SG: {ssh_access_context['app_sg']}") print(f"VPN CIDR: {ssh_access_context['vpn_cidr']}") # SSH access details ssh_config = { 'port': 22, 'key_file': '~/.ssh/django-app.pem', 'user': 'ec2-user', 'auth_method': 'Key pair authentication' } print(f"\nSSH configuration:") print(f" Port: {ssh_config['port']}") print(f" User: {ssh_config['user']}") print(f" Auth: {ssh_config['auth_method']}") print(f" Key file: {ssh_config['key_file']}") # Team members with access team_members = { 'DevOps': ['John', 'Sarah', 'Mike'], 'Backend': ['Alex', 'Emma'], 'Deployment': ['DevOps team'] } print(f"\nTeam members with SSH access:") total_members = sum(len(v) for v in team_members.values()) print(f" Total: {total_members} team members") # SSH use cases ssh_use_cases = { 'log_viewing': 'tail -f /var/log/django/app.log', 'migrations': './manage.py migrate --settings=production', 'restart_service': 'sudo systemctl restart django-app', 'check_processes': 'ps aux | grep gunicorn', 'disk_check': 'df -h && du -sh /app/*' } print(f"\nCommon SSH administration tasks:") for task, command in ssh_use_cases.items(): print(f" {task}: {command}") # Security considerations security = { 'ip_restriction': 'Limited to VPN CIDR only', 'key_based_auth': 'Passwords disabled, key pairs required', 'audit_logging': 'All SSH sessions logged to CloudWatch', 'session_timeout': '30 minutes idle timeout' } print(f"\nSecurity considerations:") for item, detail in security.items(): print(f" - {item}: {detail}") # Connection example connection_example = f"ssh -i {ssh_config['key_file']} {ssh_config['user']}@" print(f"\nConnection example:") print(f" {connection_example}") print(f"\n✓ SSH access configured from team VPN") print(f"✓ Administration and troubleshooting enabled")
Example 5: Allow Health Check Traffic from ALB to Django Instances

Add inbound rule to web app security group allowing ALB health check traffic on Django application port.

#!/bin/bash
# Allow ALB health check traffic to Django instances
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
ALB_SG="sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1"
DJANGO_PORT="8000"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Adding ALB health check access rule..."

# Add rule allowing ALB to reach Django app
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port $DJANGO_PORT \
  --source-group-id $ALB_SG \
  --description "ALB health checks and traffic to Django app" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ ALB → Django traffic rule added"
echo ""
echo "Traffic flow now configured:"
echo "  Internet → ALB (port 80/443) → Django (port 8000)"
echo ""
echo "ALB features enabled:"
echo "  - Health checks every 30 seconds"
echo "  - Load balancing across Django instances"
echo "  - Connection draining on instance shutdown"
echo "  - Sticky sessions support"
echo ""
echo "Status:"
echo "  Django instances: Ready to receive traffic"
echo "  ALB Target group: Register instances to enable"
Adding ALB health check access rule... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-alb123456789cd", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 8000, "ToPort": 8000, "ReferencedGroupInfo": { "GroupId": "sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1" }, "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ ALB → Django traffic rule added Health check traffic route confirmed
# Allow ALB health checks to Django app from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from django.http import JsonResponse alb_health_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'alb_sg': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1', 'app_port': 8000 } print(f"ALB Health Check Access Rule") print(f"App SG: {alb_health_context['app_sg']}") print(f"ALB SG: {alb_health_context['alb_sg']}") # Health check configuration health_check_config = { 'protocol': 'HTTP', 'port': 8000, 'path': '/health/', 'interval': 30, 'timeout': 5, 'healthy_threshold': 2, 'unhealthy_threshold': 2 } print(f"\nHealth check configuration:") print(f" Protocol: {health_check_config['protocol']}") print(f" Port: {health_check_config['port']}") print(f" Path: {health_check_config['path']}") print(f" Interval: {health_check_config['interval']}s") print(f" Timeout: {health_check_config['timeout']}s") # Health check responses health_metrics = { 'healthy_instances': 2, 'unhealthy_instances': 0, 'check_status': 'All passing', 'last_check': timezone.now().isoformat() } print(f"\nHealth check status:") print(f" Healthy instances: {health_metrics['healthy_instances']}") print(f" Unhealthy instances: {health_metrics['unhealthy_instances']}") print(f" Overall status: {health_metrics['check_status']}") # Health check endpoint example health_response = { 'status': 'healthy', 'database': 'connected', 'cache': 'connected', 'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(), 'uptime_seconds': 3600, 'version': '1.0.0' } print(f"\nHealth endpoint response ({health_check_config['path']}):") print(f" Status: {health_response['status']}") print(f" Database: {health_response['database']}") print(f" Cache: {health_response['cache']}") # Application readiness for traffic app_readiness = { 'can_handle_requests': True, 'database_accessible': True, 'cache_accessible': True, 'disk_space_ok': True, 'memory_available': True } print(f"\nApplication readiness:") for check, result in app_readiness.items(): status = '✓' if result else '✗' print(f" {status} {check}") # Load balancer target group target_group = { 'name': 'django-app', 'health_check_path': health_check_config['path'], 'target_type': 'instance', 'registered_targets': health_metrics['healthy_instances'] } print(f"\nTarget group configuration:") print(f" Name: {target_group['name']}") print(f" Registered targets: {target_group['registered_targets']}") print(f" Health check path: {target_group['health_check_path']}") print(f"\n✓ ALB can reach Django instances") print(f"✓ Health checks passing")

authorize-security-group-egress

Adds outbound traffic rules to a security group, controlling what traffic instances can send out. By default, all outbound traffic is allowed, but you can restrict it to specific destinations (databases, APIs, cache systems) for enhanced security. Essential for Django apps communicating with RDS, Redis, external APIs, and package repositories.

Syntax
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id {sg-id} \
  --protocol {tcp|udp|icmp|-1} \
  --port {port-number} \
  --cidr {cidr-block} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --group-id (string, required): Security group ID to add rule to (sg-xxxxxxxxx)
  • --protocol (string, required): Protocol type - tcp, udp, icmp, or -1 for all
  • --port (string): Port number or port range (e.g., 443 for HTTPS, 3306 for MySQL)
  • --cidr (string): CIDR block for destination (e.g., 0.0.0.0/0 for all, 10.0.0.0/16 for VPC)
  • --destination-security-group-id (string): Allow traffic to another security group
  • --description (string): Description of what this rule allows
Return Value
  • Return (boolean): True if rule was successfully added
  • SecurityGroupRules (Array): Details of the rule that was added
5 Detailed EC2 authorize-security-group-egress Examples
Example 1: Allow Django Instances to Access External APIs (HTTPS Only)

Restrict outbound traffic to HTTPS on port 443 for calling third-party APIs (payment, email, authentication services).

#!/bin/bash
# Allow Django instances to call external APIs via HTTPS
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Configuring outbound HTTPS access for external APIs..."

# First, remove the default allow-all outbound rule
echo "Removing default allow-all outbound rule..."
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --ip-permissions IpProtocol=-1,IpRanges='[{CidrIp=0.0.0.0/0}]' \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Default rule removed"

# Add specific HTTPS rule for external APIs
echo "Adding HTTPS (port 443) outbound rule..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 443 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --description "HTTPS to external APIs (payment, email, auth services)" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ HTTPS outbound rule added"

# Also allow DNS (port 53) for domain lookups
echo "Adding DNS (port 53) outbound rule..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol udp \
  --port 53 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --description "DNS for domain name resolution" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ DNS rule added"
echo ""
echo "Outbound access configured:"
echo "  ✓ HTTPS (443) to external APIs"
echo "  ✓ DNS (53) for domain resolution"
echo "  ✗ HTTP (80) blocked for security"
echo "  ✗ SSH (22) blocked"
echo ""
echo "Django instances can now:"
echo "  - Call payment APIs (Stripe, PayPal)"
echo "  - Send emails via SES/SendGrid"
echo "  - Authenticate with OAuth providers"
echo "  - Download packages from PyPI (port 443)"
Configuring outbound HTTPS access for external APIs... Removing default allow-all outbound rule... ✓ Default rule removed Adding HTTPS (port 443) outbound rule... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-egress-https", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ HTTPS outbound rule added Adding DNS (port 53) outbound rule... ✓ DNS rule added Outbound access configured: ✓ HTTPS (443) to external APIs ✓ DNS (53) for domain resolution
# Django outbound HTTPS access for external APIs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import requests egress_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'protocols_allowed': ['HTTPS (443)', 'DNS (53)'], 'restrictions': 'No HTTP, SSH, or arbitrary port access' } print(f"Django Outbound Access - External APIs") print(f"App SG: {egress_context['app_sg']}") # External APIs that Django calls external_apis = { 'stripe_payment': { 'url': 'https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges', 'port': 443, 'purpose': 'Process job application payments', 'protocol': 'HTTPS' }, 'sendgrid_email': { 'url': 'https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send', 'port': 443, 'purpose': 'Send job notifications to authors', 'protocol': 'HTTPS' }, 'oauth_google': { 'url': 'https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token', 'port': 443, 'purpose': 'Google Sign-In authentication', 'protocol': 'HTTPS' }, 'dns_resolution': { 'url': 'DNS queries', 'port': 53, 'purpose': 'Resolve domain names', 'protocol': 'UDP' } } print(f"\nExternal APIs accessible:") for api_name, api_config in external_apis.items(): print(f" - {api_config['purpose']}") print(f" URL: {api_config['url']}") print(f" Port: {api_config['port']}") # Example API calls from Django api_calls = { 'charge_payment': 'stripe.Charge.create(amount=10000, currency="usd")', 'send_email': 'SendGridAPIClient(SENDGRID_API_KEY).send(message)', 'oauth_token': 'requests.post("https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token", ...)', 'domain_lookup': 'socket.getaddrinfo("example.com", 443)' } print(f"\nExample Django API calls (now working):") for call_name, code in api_calls.items(): print(f" {code}") # Security profile security = { 'allowed_protocols': ['TCP:443 (HTTPS)', 'UDP:53 (DNS)'], 'blocked_protocols': ['HTTP:80', 'SSH:22', 'Unencrypted protocols'], 'encryption': 'All traffic encrypted (TLS/SSL)', 'api_access': 'Restricted to secure endpoints only' } print(f"\nSecurity profile:") print(f" Allowed: {', '.join(security['allowed_protocols'])}") print(f" Blocked: {', '.join(security['blocked_protocols'])}") print(f" Encryption: {security['encryption']}") # Outbound traffic metrics traffic_metrics = { 'outbound_requests_per_minute': 250, 'avg_response_time_ms': 450, 'api_success_rate': 99.5, 'failed_requests': 1 } print(f"\nExpected traffic:") print(f" Requests/min: {traffic_metrics['outbound_requests_per_minute']}") print(f" Success rate: {traffic_metrics['api_success_rate']}%") print(f" Response time: {traffic_metrics['avg_response_time_ms']}ms average") print(f"\n✓ Secure outbound access configured") print(f"✓ Django can call external APIs")
✓ MySQL outbound rule added Database access configured: Source: django-web-app (sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8) Destination: django-database (sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9) Protocol: TCP port 3306
# Django outbound access to MySQL database from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db import connections from django.utils import timezone db_egress_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'db_sg': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'port': 3306 } print(f"Django Outbound - Database Access") print(f"App SG: {db_egress_context['app_sg']}") print(f"DB SG: {db_egress_context['db_sg']}") # Database connection configuration db_config = { 'host': 'django-db.c1a2b3c4d5e6.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com', 'port': 3306, 'user': 'admin', 'database': 'anihpj_jobpost', 'charset': 'utf8mb4' } print(f"\nDatabase connection details:") print(f" Host: {db_config['host']}") print(f" Port: {db_config['port']}") print(f" Database: {db_config['database']}") # Django ORM operations now possible orm_operations = { 'create': 'JobPost.objects.create(title="Dev", status="published")', 'read': 'JobPost.objects.filter(status="published").count()', 'update': 'author.profile.bio = "Updated"; author.save()', 'delete': 'expired_post.delete()' } print(f"\nDjango ORM operations (now working):") for op_type, code in orm_operations.items(): print(f" {op_type}: {code}") # Expected database queries query_stats = { 'queries_per_second': 50, 'avg_query_time_ms': 25, 'max_connections': 100, 'pool_size': 10 } print(f"\nDatabase query statistics:") print(f" Expected QPS: {query_stats['queries_per_second']}") print(f" Connection pool: {query_stats['pool_size']}") print(f" Max connections: {query_stats['max_connections']}") # Models accessing database models_accessing = { 'JobPost': 'Job listings and details', 'Author': 'Job post authors', 'Skills': 'Required job skills', 'Location': 'Job locations', 'User': 'User accounts' } print(f"\nModels with outbound database access:") for model, purpose in models_accessing.items(): print(f" - {model}: {purpose}") # Data flow data_flow = { 'read_operations': 'Retrieve jobs, skills, authors from DB', 'write_operations': 'Create new job posts, applications', 'search_operations': 'Full-text search, filtering, pagination', 'transaction_operations': 'Multi-model atomic operations' } print(f"\nData flow enabled:") for flow_type, description in data_flow.items(): print(f" - {description}") print(f"\n✓ Outbound database connection configured") print(f"✓ Django models fully functional")
Example 3: Allow Django to Access Redis Cache (Outbound)

Configure outbound access from Django instances to Redis cache for session storage and query caching.

#!/bin/bash
# Allow Django instances outbound access to Redis cache
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
CACHE_SG="sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Configuring Django outbound access to Redis cache..."

# Add Redis outbound rule
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 6379 \
  --destination-security-group-id $CACHE_SG \
  --description "Redis cache access (outbound)" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Redis outbound rule added"
echo ""
echo "Cache access configured:"
echo "  Source: django-web-app ($APP_SG)"
echo "  Destination: django-cache ($CACHE_SG)"
echo "  Protocol: TCP"
echo "  Port: 6379 (Redis)"
echo ""
echo "Django can now:"
echo "  - Store user sessions in Redis"
echo "  - Cache query results"
echo "  - Queue background tasks"
echo "  - Implement rate limiting"
Configuring Django outbound access to Redis cache... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-egress-redis", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 6379, "ToPort": 6379, "ReferencedGroupInfo": { "GroupId": "sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0" }, "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ Redis outbound rule added Cache access configured
# Django outbound access to Redis cache from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.cache import cache from django.contrib.sessions.models import Session from django.utils import timezone cache_egress_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'cache_sg': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'port': 6379 } print(f"Django Outbound - Cache Access") print(f"App SG: {cache_egress_context['app_sg']}") print(f"Cache SG: {cache_egress_context['cache_sg']}") # Cache connection configuration cache_config = { 'host': 'django-cache.c1a2b3c4d5e6.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com', 'port': 6379, 'type': 'ElastiCache Redis', 'max_connections': 100 } print(f"\nCache connection details:") print(f" Type: {cache_config['type']}") print(f" Port: {cache_config['port']}") print(f" Max connections: {cache_config['max_connections']}") # Cache operations now possible cache_operations = { 'set_session': 'cache.set(f"session:{user_id}", session_data, 86400)', 'get_jobs': 'cache.get(f"jobs:page:{page_num}")', 'cache_authors': 'cache.set("authors:all", authors_list, 3600)', 'delete_cache': 'cache.delete(f"skills:user:{user_id}")' } print(f"\nCache operations (now working):") for op_name, code in cache_operations.items(): print(f" {code}") # Cache performance metrics cache_metrics = { 'set_ops_per_sec': 200, 'get_ops_per_sec': 500, 'hit_rate': 85, 'response_time_ms': 2 } print(f"\nCache performance:") print(f" SET operations: {cache_metrics['set_ops_per_sec']}/sec") print(f" GET operations: {cache_metrics['get_ops_per_sec']}/sec") print(f" Hit rate: {cache_metrics['hit_rate']}%") print(f" Response time: {cache_metrics['response_time_ms']}ms") # Data cached from Django models cached_models = { 'JobPost': 'Published jobs (1 hour TTL)', 'Author': 'Author profiles (2 hours TTL)', 'Skills': 'All skills list (12 hours TTL)', 'Location': 'Unique locations (24 hours TTL)', 'Sessions': 'User sessions (24 hours TTL)' } print(f"\nData cached:") for model, ttl in cached_models.items(): print(f" - {model}: {ttl}") # Background tasks using cache celery_tasks = { 'index_jobs': 'Cache new job listings for search', 'update_skills': 'Refresh skill cache', 'cleanup_sessions': 'Remove expired sessions', 'report_generation': 'Cache report data' } print(f"\nBackground tasks using cache:") for task, purpose in celery_tasks.items(): print(f" - {task}: {purpose}") print(f"\n✓ Outbound Redis access configured") print(f"✓ Session and query caching enabled")
Example 4: Allow Package Downloads from PyPI and APT Repositories

Configure outbound access for package manager operations (pip, apt-get) to download dependencies.

#!/bin/bash
# Allow package downloads from PyPI and system repositories
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Configuring outbound access for package repositories..."

# HTTPS for PyPI and APT (port 443)
echo "Adding HTTPS (443) for PyPI..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 443 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --description "HTTPS for PyPI, APT, and package downloads" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ HTTPS rule added"

# HTTP for some repositories (port 80 - if needed)
echo "Adding HTTP (80) for package repositories..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 80 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --description "HTTP for package repository mirrors" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ HTTP rule added"
echo ""
echo "Package management configured:"
echo "  PyPI (pip): https://pypi.org/"
echo "  Ubuntu APT: https://archive.ubuntu.com/"
echo "  Debian repos: https://deb.debian.org/"
echo ""
echo "Instances can now:"
echo "  - pip install Django packages"
echo "  - apt-get update and install"
echo "  - Download security patches"
Configuring outbound access for package repositories... Adding HTTPS (443) for PyPI... ✓ HTTPS rule added { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-egress-pypi", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } Adding HTTP (80) for package repositories... ✓ HTTP rule added Package management configured
# Package repository outbound access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import subprocess package_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'protocols': ['HTTP (80)', 'HTTPS (443)'], 'purpose': 'Package downloads and OS updates' } print(f"Package Repository Access - Outbound") print(f"App SG: {package_context['app_sg']}") # Python dependencies installed via pip python_packages = { 'Django': '5.2.5', 'Django-REST-framework': '3.14.0', 'psycopg2-binary': '2.9.9', 'redis': '5.0.1', 'celery': '5.3.4', 'gunicorn': '21.2.0', 'requests': '2.31.0' } print(f"\nPython packages installed via pip:") print(f" Total packages: {len(python_packages)}") for package, version in list(python_packages.items())[:5]: print(f" - {package}: {version}") # System packages via apt-get system_packages = { 'python3': 'Python runtime', 'postgresql-client': 'Database client', 'git': 'Version control', 'curl': 'HTTP client', 'htop': 'System monitoring' } print(f"\nSystem packages via apt-get:") for package, purpose in system_packages.items(): print(f" - {package}: {purpose}") # Repository endpoints accessed repositories = { 'pypi': 'https://pypi.org/pypi - Python Package Index', 'ubuntu': 'https://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu - Ubuntu packages', 'debian': 'https://deb.debian.org/debian - Debian packages', 'security': 'https://security.ubuntu.com - Security updates' } print(f"\nRepositories accessed (outbound):") for repo, url in repositories.items(): print(f" - {repo}: {url}") # Package installation commands now working install_commands = { 'pip_install': 'pip install -r requirements.txt', 'apt_update': 'apt-get update', 'apt_install': 'apt-get install -y postgresql-client', 'security_update': 'apt-get upgrade' } print(f"\nCommands now working:") for cmd, example in install_commands.items(): print(f" - {example}") # Dependency management dependencies = { 'django_deps': 20, 'system_deps': 15, 'security_patches': 5, 'total_size_mb': 450 } print(f"\nDependency statistics:") print(f" Python packages: {dependencies['django_deps']}") print(f" System packages: {dependencies['system_deps']}") print(f" Total download: {dependencies['total_size_mb']}MB") print(f"\n✓ Package repository access configured") print(f"✓ Python and system dependencies installable")
Example 5: Restrict Outbound to Internal VPC Only (Zero-Trust Network)

Implement zero-trust networking by removing default all-allow rule and only allowing traffic to internal VPC resources.

#!/bin/bash
# Implement zero-trust: restrict outbound to VPC only
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
VPC_CIDR="10.0.0.0/16"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Implementing zero-trust network security..."
echo "Removing default allow-all outbound rule..."

# Remove default allow-all rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --ip-permissions IpProtocol=-1,IpRanges='[{CidrIp=0.0.0.0/0}]' \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Default rule removed"

# Add specific outbound rules for internal services only

# Allow traffic to database
echo "Adding outbound to database security group..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 3306 \
  --cidr 10.0.2.0/24 \
  --description "MySQL database (private subnet)" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Database rule added"

# Allow traffic to cache
echo "Adding outbound to cache security group..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 6379 \
  --cidr 10.0.3.0/24 \
  --description "Redis cache (private subnet)" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Cache rule added"

# Allow DNS within VPC
echo "Adding outbound DNS within VPC..."
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol udp \
  --port 53 \
  --cidr $VPC_CIDR \
  --description "DNS within VPC" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ DNS rule added"

echo ""
echo "Zero-trust security configured:"
echo "  ✗ No internet access"
echo "  ✓ Only internal VPC traffic allowed"
echo "  ✓ Database access: 10.0.2.0/24"
echo "  ✓ Cache access: 10.0.3.0/24"
echo "  ✓ DNS within VPC: 10.0.0.0/16"
Implementing zero-trust network security... Removing default allow-all outbound rule... ✓ Default rule removed Adding outbound to database security group... ✓ Database rule added { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-vpc-db", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 3306, "ToPort": 3306, "CidrIpv4": "10.0.2.0/24", "IsEgress": true }] } Adding outbound to cache security group... ✓ Cache rule added Adding outbound DNS within VPC... ✓ DNS rule added Zero-trust security configured
# Zero-trust outbound network access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone zerotrust_context = { 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'vpc_cidr': '10.0.0.0/16', 'security_model': 'Zero-trust (deny all, allow specific)' } print(f"Zero-Trust Network Configuration") print(f"App SG: {zerotrust_context['app_sg']}") print(f"Security model: {zerotrust_context['security_model']}") # Allowed outbound destinations allowed_destinations = { 'database': { 'cidr': '10.0.2.0/24', 'port': 3306, 'protocol': 'TCP', 'service': 'MySQL RDS' }, 'cache': { 'cidr': '10.0.3.0/24', 'port': 6379, 'protocol': 'TCP', 'service': 'Redis ElastiCache' }, 'dns': { 'cidr': '10.0.0.0/16', 'port': 53, 'protocol': 'UDP', 'service': 'Route53 DNS' } } print(f"\nAllowed outbound destinations:") for dest_name, dest_config in allowed_destinations.items(): print(f" - {dest_config['service']}") print(f" CIDR: {dest_config['cidr']}") print(f" Port: {dest_config['port']}") # Blocked outbound traffic blocked_traffic = { 'internet': 'No 0.0.0.0/0 (Internet)', 'external_apis': 'No external API access', 'package_repos': 'No PyPI or apt-get', 'dns_external': 'No external DNS (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1)' } print(f"\nBlocked outbound traffic (zero-trust):") for blocked_type, reason in blocked_traffic.items(): print(f" ✗ {blocked_type}: {reason}") # Network topology network_topology = { 'app_layer': '10.0.1.0/24 - Django instances', 'database_layer': '10.0.2.0/24 - RDS MySQL', 'cache_layer': '10.0.3.0/24 - Redis', 'management_layer': '10.0.0.0/24 - Bastion/VPN' } print(f"\nVPC network topology:") for layer, cidr in network_topology.items(): print(f" - {layer}: {cidr}") # Available operations operations = { 'database_queries': 'SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE', 'cache_operations': 'SET, GET, DELETE, EXPIRE', 'service_discovery': 'Internal DNS resolution', 'external_apis': 'NOT ALLOWED - Network blocked' } print(f"\nAllowed operations:") for op, desc in operations.items(): if 'NOT' not in desc: print(f" ✓ {desc}") else: print(f" ✗ {desc}") # Security benefits security_benefits = { 'data_exfiltration': 'Prevented - no internet access', 'malware_c2': 'Blocked - no external callbacks', 'lateral_movement': 'Restricted - only specific destinations', 'compliance': 'PCI-DSS, HIPAA compatible' } print(f"\nSecurity benefits:") for benefit, detail in security_benefits.items(): print(f" ✓ {detail}") print(f"\n✓ Zero-trust network security implemented") print(f"✓ Only internal VPC traffic allowed")
{ "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-egress-mysql", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 3306, "ToPort": 3306, "ReferencedGroupInfo": { "GroupId": "sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9", "GroupOwnerId": "123456789012" }, "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ MySQL outbound rule added Database access configured: Source: django-web-app (sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8) Destination: django-database (sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9)

describe-security-groups

Retrieve information about security groups, including details about their rules, tags, and associated resources. Useful for querying security group configuration and verifying network access rules.

Syntax
aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  [--group-ids ] \
  [--group-names ] \
  [--filters Name=,Values=] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --group-ids (list): One or more security group IDs to describe. When specified, only these groups are returned. Multiple IDs can be provided as space-separated values.
  • --group-names (list): One or more security group names to describe. This only works for security groups in the default VPC. For VPC security groups, use --group-ids instead.
  • --filters (list): Filter results by specific attributes. Common filters include group-name, vpc-id, tag-key, tag-value, group-id. Format: Name=key,Values=value1 value2
  • --region (string): AWS region where the security groups are located (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of results to return per page (default 1000)
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination to retrieve next batch of results
Return Value
  • SecurityGroups (Array): List of security group objects with detailed configuration
  • GroupId (String): Unique security group identifier (sg-xxxxxxxx)
  • GroupName (String): Human-readable name of the security group
  • VpcId (String): VPC containing the security group
  • IpPermissions (Array): Inbound rules for the security group
  • IpPermissionsEgress (Array): Outbound rules for the security group
  • Tags (Array): Key-value tags associated with the security group
  • OwnerId (String): AWS account ID that owns the security group
Example 1: List All Security Groups in VPC

Retrieve all security groups in a specific VPC and display their details for auditing and verification.

#!/bin/bash
# List all security groups in a VPC
VPC_ID="vpc-12345678"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Listing all security groups in VPC: $VPC_ID"
echo ""

aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --filters Name=vpc-id,Values=$VPC_ID \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupId,GroupName,VpcId]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Query results:"
echo "  - GroupId: Unique security group identifier"
echo "  - GroupName: Human-readable name"
echo "  - VpcId: VPC containing the security group"
Listing all security groups in VPC: vpc-12345678 +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ | GroupId | GroupName | VpcId | +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ | sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h | django-web-app | vpc-12345678 | | sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h | django-database | vpc-12345678 | | sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i | django-cache | vpc-12345678 | | sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j | django-alb | vpc-12345678 | | sg-4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k | django-dev | vpc-12345678 | +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ Query results: - GroupId: Unique security group identifier - GroupName: Human-readable name - VpcId: VPC containing the security group
# List all security groups in VPC from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone sg_list = { 'django-web-app': { 'sg_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-12345678', 'purpose': 'Django application servers', 'inbound_rules': 3, 'outbound_rules': 5 }, 'django-database': { 'sg_id': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-12345678', 'purpose': 'RDS MySQL database', 'inbound_rules': 2, 'outbound_rules': 1 }, 'django-cache': { 'sg_id': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-12345678', 'purpose': 'ElastiCache Redis', 'inbound_rules': 1, 'outbound_rules': 2 }, 'django-alb': { 'sg_id': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-12345678', 'purpose': 'Application Load Balancer', 'inbound_rules': 2, 'outbound_rules': 3 } } print(f"Security Groups in VPC (vpc-12345678)") print(f"Total security groups: {len(sg_list)}") print(f"") for sg_name, sg_info in sg_list.items(): print(f"Security Group: {sg_name}") print(f" ID: {sg_info['sg_id']}") print(f" Purpose: {sg_info['purpose']}") print(f" Inbound rules: {sg_info['inbound_rules']}") print(f" Outbound rules: {sg_info['outbound_rules']}") print(f"") # Django deployment context deployment_context = { 'environment': 'production', 'app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'db_sg': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'cache_sg': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'alb_sg': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'instances': 5, 'databases': 2, 'caches': 1 } print(f"Django Deployment Network Topology") print(f"Environment: {deployment_context['environment']}") print(f"Total instances: {deployment_context['instances']}") print(f"RDS instances: {deployment_context['databases']}") print(f"Cache clusters: {deployment_context['caches']}") print(f"") # Access patterns access_patterns = { 'alb_to_app': 'ALB (80/443) → Django app (8000)', 'app_to_db': 'Django app (ephemeral) → RDS (3306)', 'app_to_cache': 'Django app (ephemeral) → Redis (6379)', 'internet_to_alb': 'Internet (0.0.0.0/0) → ALB (80/443)' } print(f"Network access patterns:") for pattern, flow in access_patterns.items(): print(f" - {flow}") print(f"\n✓ Security group configuration retrieved") print(f"✓ Network topology verified")
Example 2: Get Security Group Details with Rules

Retrieve detailed information about a specific security group including all inbound and outbound rules.

#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed security group configuration
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Security Group Details: $APP_SG"
echo ""

# Get security group details in JSON format
aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --group-ids $APP_SG \
  --region $REGION

echo ""
echo "Details retrieved:"
echo "  - Security group name and ID"
echo "  - VPC and owner information"
echo "  - All inbound rules (IpPermissions)"
echo "  - All outbound rules (IpPermissionsEgress)"
echo "  - Associated tags"
Security Group Details: sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 { "SecurityGroups": [{ "IpPermissionsEgress": [ { "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "IpRanges": [{ "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0", "Description": "HTTPS outbound" }] } ], "GroupName": "django-web-app", "VpcId": "vpc-12345678", "IpPermissions": [ { "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 8000, "ToPort": 8000, "UserIdGroupPairs": [{ "GroupId": "sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j", "Description": "ALB to Django app" }] } ], "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "OwnerId": "123456789012", "GroupDescription": "Django application security group", "Tags": [{ "Key": "Name", "Value": "django-web-app" }] }] }
# Get security group details with all rules from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost sg_details = { 'group_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'group_name': 'django-web-app', 'vpc_id': 'vpc-12345678', 'owner_id': '123456789012', 'description': 'Django application security group' } print(f"Security Group Configuration") print(f"Name: {sg_details['group_name']}") print(f"ID: {sg_details['group_id']}") print(f"VPC: {sg_details['vpc_id']}") print(f"Description: {sg_details['description']}") print(f"") # Inbound rules inbound_rules = [ { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 8000, 'source': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'source_name': 'ALB', 'description': 'ALB to Django app' }, { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 22, 'source': '203.0.113.0/32', 'source_name': 'VPN Gateway', 'description': 'SSH from VPN' }, { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 443, 'source': '0.0.0.0/0', 'source_name': 'Public internet', 'description': 'HTTPS (via ALB)' } ] print(f"Inbound Rules ({len(inbound_rules)} total)") for rule_idx, rule in enumerate(inbound_rules, 1): print(f" {rule_idx}. {rule['protocol']} port {rule['port']}") print(f" Source: {rule['source']} ({rule['source_name']})") print(f" Description: {rule['description']}") print(f"") # Outbound rules outbound_rules = [ { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 3306, 'destination': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'service': 'MySQL', 'description': 'Database access' }, { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 6379, 'destination': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'service': 'Redis', 'description': 'Cache access' }, { 'protocol': 'TCP', 'port': 443, 'destination': '0.0.0.0/0', 'service': 'HTTPS', 'description': 'External APIs' }, { 'protocol': 'UDP', 'port': 53, 'destination': '10.0.0.0/16', 'service': 'DNS', 'description': 'VPC DNS' } ] print(f"Outbound Rules ({len(outbound_rules)} total)") for rule_idx, rule in enumerate(outbound_rules, 1): print(f" {rule_idx}. {rule['protocol']} port {rule['port']}") print(f" Destination: {rule['destination']}") print(f" Service: {rule['service']}") print(f"") # Access control summary access_summary = { 'http_https': 'Allowed from ALB', 'ssh': 'Allowed from VPN only', 'database': 'Outbound to RDS only', 'cache': 'Outbound to ElastiCache only', 'apis': 'Outbound HTTPS to internet', 'dns': 'Outbound to VPC DNS' } print(f"Access Control Summary") for access_type, rule in access_summary.items(): print(f" {access_type}: {rule}") print(f"\n✓ Security group rules audited") print(f"✓ Network access patterns verified")
Example 3: Filter Security Groups by Tag

Find security groups using tag filters to identify resources by environment, application, or cost center.

#!/bin/bash
# Filter security groups by tag
REGION="us-east-1"
TAG_KEY="Environment"
TAG_VALUE="Production"

echo "Finding security groups tagged: $TAG_KEY=$TAG_VALUE"
echo ""

aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --filters "Name=tag:$TAG_KEY,Values=$TAG_VALUE" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupId,GroupName,Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Common tags used for filtering:"
echo "  - Environment: Production, Staging, Development"
echo "  - Application: anihpj, api, worker"
echo "  - Team: backend, devops, security"
echo "  - CostCenter: engineering, operations"
Finding security groups tagged: Environment=Production +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ | GroupId | GroupName | Name | +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ | sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h | django-web-app | django-web-app | | sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h | django-database | django-database | | sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i | django-cache | django-cache | | sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j | django-alb | django-alb | +---------------------+---------------------+---------------------+ Common tags used for filtering
# Filter security groups by tag from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone tagged_sgs = { 'django-web-app': { 'group_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'environment': 'Production', 'application': 'anihpj-jobpost', 'team': 'backend', 'cost_center': 'engineering' }, 'django-database': { 'group_id': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'environment': 'Production', 'application': 'anihpj-jobpost', 'team': 'devops', 'cost_center': 'infrastructure' }, 'django-cache': { 'group_id': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'environment': 'Production', 'application': 'anihpj-jobpost', 'team': 'devops', 'cost_center': 'infrastructure' }, 'django-alb': { 'group_id': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'environment': 'Production', 'application': 'anihpj-jobpost', 'team': 'backend', 'cost_center': 'networking' } } print(f"Security Groups - Production Environment") print(f"Total tagged: {len(tagged_sgs)}") print(f"") for sg_name, sg_tags in tagged_sgs.items(): print(f"{sg_name}:") print(f" Group ID: {sg_tags['group_id']}") print(f" Environment: {sg_tags['environment']}") print(f" Application: {sg_tags['application']}") print(f" Team: {sg_tags['team']}") print(f" Cost Center: {sg_tags['cost_center']}") print(f"") # Tag filtering context filter_context = { 'query_tag': 'Environment=Production', 'total_sgs': 4, 'instances': 10, 'databases': 2, 'caches': 1, 'load_balancers': 1 } print(f"Filter Results Summary") print(f"Query: {filter_context['query_tag']}") print(f"Total resources protected by tags: {sum([filter_context['instances'], filter_context['databases']])}") print(f"") # Security group purposes purposes = { 'django-web-app': 'Application servers (5 instances)', 'django-database': 'RDS database (2 instances)', 'django-cache': 'ElastiCache (1 cluster)', 'django-alb': 'Application load balancer (1 ALB)' } print(f"Protected Resources") for sg_name, purpose in purposes.items(): print(f" - {purpose}") print(f"\n✓ Tag-based filtering successful") print(f"✓ Production environment security groups identified")
Example 4: Check Inbound Rules for Specific Port

Query security groups to find which ones allow traffic on a specific port for compliance and security audits.

#!/bin/bash
# Find security groups allowing specific port
PORT="3306"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Finding security groups allowing inbound port $PORT (MySQL)..."
echo ""

aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --filters "Name=ip-permission.cidr,Values=0.0.0.0/0" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupId,GroupName,IpPermissions[?FromPort==`'$PORT'`].FromPort]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Port analysis:"
echo "  Searched port: $PORT (MySQL)"
echo "  Filter: Open to 0.0.0.0/0 (public internet)"
echo ""
echo "WARNING: Database port should NOT be open to public!"
echo "Recommended: Allow only from app security group"
Finding security groups allowing inbound port 3306 (MySQL)... +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ | GroupId | GroupName | Port | +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ | sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h | django-database | 3306 | +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ Port analysis: Searched port: 3306 (MySQL) Filter: Open to 0.0.0.0/0 Security check results: ✓ MySQL port protected (only from app SG) ✓ No public internet access ✓ Restricted to application instances
# Check inbound rules for port 3306 from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone port_audit = { 'port_checked': 3306, 'service': 'MySQL', 'sgs_checked': 5, 'sgs_allowing': 1 } print(f"Security Audit - Port {port_audit['port_checked']} Analysis") print(f"Service: {port_audit['service']}") print(f"") # MySQL security rules mysql_rules = { 'django-database': { 'sg_id': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'port': 3306, 'source': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'source_name': 'django-web-app', 'public_access': False, 'compliance': 'PASSED' } } print(f"Security Groups with port {port_audit['port_checked']} rules") for sg_name, rule in mysql_rules.items(): print(f" {sg_name}:") print(f" ID: {rule['sg_id']}") print(f" Source: {rule['source']} ({rule['source_name']})") print(f" Public access: {rule['public_access']}") print(f" Compliance: {rule['compliance']}") print(f"") # Port exposure analysis port_exposure = { 'port': 3306, 'total_sgs': 5, 'sgs_open_to_internet': 0, 'sgs_restricted': 1, 'security_status': 'SECURE' } print(f"Port Exposure Analysis") print(f"Total security groups: {port_exposure['total_sgs']}") print(f"Exposed to 0.0.0.0/0: {port_exposure['sgs_open_to_internet']}") print(f"Properly restricted: {port_exposure['sgs_restricted']}") print(f"Security status: {port_exposure['security_status']}") print(f"") # Best practices validation best_practices = { 'principle_of_least_privilege': 'PASSED - Only app SG can access', 'no_world_access': 'PASSED - 0.0.0.0/0 not allowed', 'specific_port': 'PASSED - Port 3306 only', 'description_present': 'PASSED - Rule documented' } print(f"Security Best Practices Check") for practice, result in best_practices.items(): status = '✓' if 'PASSED' in result else '✗' print(f" {status} {practice}: {result}") # Django database access db_access_stats = { 'connections': 10, 'concurrent_users': 50, 'queries_per_second': 150, 'avg_response_ms': 45 } print(f"\nDatabase access statistics:") print(f" Connection pool: {db_access_stats['connections']}") print(f" Concurrent users: {db_access_stats['concurrent_users']}") print(f" Queries/sec: {db_access_stats['queries_per_second']}") print(f"\n✓ Port security audit completed") print(f"✓ All compliance checks passed")
Example 5: List Security Groups with Instance Count

Get a summary of security groups showing how many instances are using each group for capacity planning.

#!/bin/bash
# Get security group usage summary
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Security Group Usage Summary"
echo ""

# Get security groups with instance count
aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupId,GroupName,length(IpPermissions)]' \
  --output table

echo ""

# Get instance counts per security group
echo "Instance count per security group:"
aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters Name=instance-state-name,Values=running \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].[SecurityGroups[0].GroupId,InstanceId]' \
  --output text | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

echo ""
echo "Usage patterns:"
echo "  - Shows which security groups are actively used"
echo "  - Identifies unused security groups for cleanup"
echo "  - Helps with capacity planning"
Security Group Usage Summary +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ | GroupId | GroupName | Rules | +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ | sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h | django-web-app | 3 | | sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h | django-database | 2 | | sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i | django-cache | 1 | | sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j | django-alb | 2 | | sg-default | default | 1 | +---------------------+---------------------+----------+ Instance count per security group: 5 sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h (django-web-app) 2 sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h (django-database) 1 sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i (django-cache) 1 sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j (django-alb) Usage patterns: - Shows which security groups are actively used - Identifies unused security groups for cleanup - Helps with capacity planning
# List security groups with instance and resource count from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from django.db.models import Count sg_usage = { 'django-web-app': { 'group_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'instances': 5, 'status': 'Active', 'cpu_utilization': 45.2, 'memory_utilization': 68.5 }, 'django-database': { 'group_id': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'instances': 2, 'status': 'Active (Primary + Standby)', 'cpu_utilization': 35.8, 'memory_utilization': 52.3 }, 'django-cache': { 'group_id': 'sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0', 'instances': 1, 'status': 'Active', 'cpu_utilization': 22.1, 'memory_utilization': 48.7 }, 'django-alb': { 'group_id': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'instances': 1, 'status': 'Active', 'cpu_utilization': 12.5, 'memory_utilization': 10.2 } } print(f"Security Group Usage Summary (Production)") print(f"") total_instances = 0 for sg_name, sg_info in sg_usage.items(): total_instances += sg_info['instances'] status_mark = '✓' if sg_info['status'] == 'Active' else '⚠' print(f"{status_mark} {sg_name}") print(f" Instances: {sg_info['instances']}") print(f" CPU: {sg_info['cpu_utilization']}%") print(f" Memory: {sg_info['memory_utilization']}%") print(f"") print(f"Total instances protected: {total_instances}") print(f"Total security groups: {len(sg_usage)}") print(f"") # Resource capacity capacity = { 'app_tier': { 'instances': 5, 'max_capacity': 10, 'utilization': 50 }, 'database_tier': { 'instances': 2, 'max_capacity': 4, 'utilization': 50 }, 'cache_tier': { 'instances': 1, 'max_capacity': 2, 'utilization': 50 } } print(f"Capacity Planning Analysis") for tier, cap_info in capacity.items(): print(f" {tier}:") print(f" Current: {cap_info['instances']} / {cap_info['max_capacity']}") print(f" Utilization: {cap_info['utilization']}%") # Django models distribution models_distribution = { 'JobPost': 1250, 'Author': 85, 'Skills': 120, 'Location': 45, 'User': 200 } print(f"\nDjango Model Data Volume") for model, count in models_distribution.items(): print(f" {model}: {count} records") print(f"\n✓ Security group usage audited") print(f"✓ Capacity planning data gathered")

revoke-security-group-ingress

Remove inbound rules from a security group. Used to revoke network access that was previously granted. Useful for restricting access, removing outdated rules, or cleaning up security group configurations.

Syntax
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id  \
  --protocol  \
  --port  \
  --cidr  | --source-security-group-id  \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --group-id (string): The ID of the security group to revoke rules from. Format: sg-xxxxxxxx
  • --protocol (string): The protocol number or name (tcp, udp, icmp, or -1 for all). Use 'tcp' for HTTP/HTTPS/MySQL, 'udp' for DNS.
  • --port (integer): The port or port range to revoke (e.g., 80, 443, 3306, 6379). Omit for protocols that don't use ports.
  • --cidr (string): The CIDR block to revoke access from (e.g., 0.0.0.0/0 for public, 10.0.0.0/8 for private VPC).
  • --source-security-group-id (string): Another security group ID instead of CIDR. Allows traffic from instances in that group.
  • --region (string): AWS region where the security group is located (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
Return Value
  • Return (boolean): True if the rule was successfully revoked, false otherwise
  • UnknownIpPermissions (Array): Rules that couldn't be found to revoke (may indicate rule already removed)
  • SecurityGroupRuleId (String): ID of the revoked rule for tracking purposes
Example 1: Revoke Public SSH Access from Security Group

Remove SSH port 22 access from all public internet (0.0.0.0/0) to tighten security and restrict administrative access.

#!/bin/bash
# Revoke public SSH access for security hardening
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Revoking public SSH access from security group..."
echo "Before: SSH (port 22) open to 0.0.0.0/0"
echo ""

# Remove the public SSH rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 22 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ SSH rule revoked"
echo ""
echo "After revocation:"
echo "  SSH access: DENIED from public internet"
echo "  SSH access: ALLOWED from VPN only (if configured)"
echo "  Security posture: IMPROVED"
Revoking public SSH access from security group... Before: SSH (port 22) open to 0.0.0.0/0 { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoked-ssh", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 22, "ToPort": 22, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ SSH rule revoked After revocation: SSH access: DENIED from public internet SSH access: ALLOWED from VPN only Security posture: IMPROVED
# Revoke public SSH access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone ssh_revoke_context = { 'group_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'port': 22, 'action': 'revoked', 'source': '0.0.0.0/0' } print(f"SSH Access Control - Revoke Operation") print(f"Security Group: {ssh_revoke_context['group_id']}") print(f"Port: {ssh_revoke_context['port']}") print(f"") # Before revocation before_config = { 'ssh_from_internet': True, 'ssh_from_vpn': True, 'ssh_from_office': True, 'public_exposure': 'HIGH RISK' } print(f"Before revocation:") print(f" SSH from 0.0.0.0/0: {before_config['ssh_from_internet']}") print(f" Public exposure: {before_config['public_exposure']}") print(f"") # After revocation after_config = { 'ssh_from_internet': False, 'ssh_from_vpn': True, 'ssh_from_office': True, 'public_exposure': 'SECURE' } print(f"After revocation:") print(f" SSH from 0.0.0.0/0: {after_config['ssh_from_internet']}") print(f" SSH from VPN (203.0.113.0/32): {after_config['ssh_from_vpn']}") print(f" Public exposure: {after_config['public_exposure']}") print(f"") # Access patterns access_patterns = { 'internet_user': 'DENIED - Cannot SSH to instances', 'vpn_admin': 'ALLOWED - Can SSH via VPN gateway', 'office_network': 'ALLOWED - Can SSH from office', 'application': 'N/A - No SSH needed from app' } print(f"Access patterns after revoke:") for pattern_type, access in access_patterns.items(): print(f" {pattern_type}: {access}") # Security improvements improvements = { 'attack_surface': 'Reduced - SSH not publicly accessible', 'brute_force': 'Prevented - No public SSH endpoint', 'compliance': 'Improved - Meets security standards', 'audit_logs': 'Will now only log VPN access' } print(f"\nSecurity improvements:") for improvement, detail in improvements.items(): print(f" ✓ {improvement}: {detail}") print(f"\n✓ SSH access revoked from public internet") print(f"✓ Security posture hardened")
Example 2: Revoke HTTP Access When Migrating to HTTPS Only

Remove HTTP port 80 access to force all traffic through HTTPS encryption as part of security hardening.

#!/bin/bash
# Revoke HTTP access - enforce HTTPS only
ALB_SG="sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Revoking HTTP access from ALB security group..."
echo "Enforcing HTTPS-only traffic"
echo ""

# Remove HTTP rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $ALB_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 80 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ HTTP rule revoked"
echo ""
echo "Traffic policies updated:"
echo "  HTTP (port 80): DISABLED"
echo "  HTTPS (port 443): REQUIRED"
echo "  ALB redirect: Enabled (80 → 443)"
echo ""
echo "Users accessing via HTTP will be redirected to HTTPS"
Revoking HTTP access from ALB security group... Enforcing HTTPS-only traffic { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoked-http", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 80, "ToPort": 80, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ HTTP rule revoked Traffic policies updated: HTTP: DISABLED HTTPS: REQUIRED
# Revoke HTTP access - enforce HTTPS only from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone http_revoke = { 'group_id': 'sg-3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j', 'port': 80, 'replacement_port': 443, 'enforcement': 'HTTPS only' } print(f"HTTP Access Revocation - HTTPS Migration") print(f"ALB Security Group: {http_revoke['group_id']}") print(f"") # Traffic patterns before before_traffic = { 'http_requests': 5000, 'https_requests': 15000, 'total_requests': 20000 } print(f"Traffic patterns before revocation:") print(f" HTTP requests: {before_traffic['http_requests']} (25%)") print(f" HTTPS requests: {before_traffic['https_requests']} (75%)") print(f" Total: {before_traffic['total_requests']}/sec") print(f"") # After revocation after_traffic = { 'http_requests': 0, 'https_requests': 20000, 'redirects': 5000, 'total_requests': 20000 } print(f"Traffic patterns after revocation:") print(f" HTTP requests: REDIRECTED to HTTPS") print(f" HTTPS requests: {after_traffic['https_requests']} (100%)") print(f" Redirect chain: HTTP 80 → HTTPS 443") print(f"") # Security benefits security_benefits = { 'encryption': 'All data encrypted in transit', 'hsts': 'HSTS header enforces HTTPS', 'certificate': 'SSL/TLS certificate required', 'vulnerability': 'No man-in-the-middle attacks' } print(f"Security enhancements:") for benefit, detail in security_benefits.items(): print(f" ✓ {benefit}: {detail}") # ALB configuration alb_config = { 'listener_80': 'Action: Redirect to HTTPS (301)', 'listener_443': 'Action: Forward to Django targets', 'certificate': 'AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)', 'protocol': 'HTTPS/TLS 1.2+' } print(f"\nALB listener configuration:") for listener, action in alb_config.items(): print(f" {listener}: {action}") # User impact user_impact = { 'direct_http': 'Automatic redirect (transparent)', 'bookmarks': 'Will work (ALB handles redirect)', 'api_clients': 'Must be updated to HTTPS', 'browser_cache': 'HSTS pins HTTPS for 1 year' } print(f"\nUser experience:") for scenario, impact in user_impact.items(): print(f" {scenario}: {impact}") print(f"\n✓ HTTP access revoked") print(f"✓ HTTPS-only enforcement active")
Example 3: Revoke Database Access from Old Application Tier

Remove database access from a legacy application security group when decommissioning old infrastructure.

#!/bin/bash
# Revoke database access from old app SG during migration
OLD_APP_SG="sg-old1a2b3c4d5e6f7g"
DB_SG="sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Revoking database access from legacy application..."
echo "Old App SG: $OLD_APP_SG"
echo "Database SG: $DB_SG"
echo ""

# Revoke MySQL access from old app security group
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $DB_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 3306 \
  --source-security-group-id $OLD_APP_SG \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Database rule revoked"
echo ""
echo "Access removed:"
echo "  Old app instances: DENIED database access"
echo "  Database security: IMPROVED"
echo "  Migration status: In progress"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Verify new app can access database"
echo "  2. Drain connections from old app instances"
echo "  3. Monitor database performance"
echo "  4. Decommission old instances"
Revoking database access from legacy application... Old App SG: sg-old1a2b3c4d5e6f7g Database SG: sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9 { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoked-db-old-app", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 3306, "ToPort": 3306, "ReferencedGroupInfo": { "GroupId": "sg-old1a2b3c4d5e6f7g" }, "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ Database rule revoked
# Revoke database access from old app tier from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone migration_context = { 'old_app_sg': 'sg-old1a2b3c4d5e6f7g', 'new_app_sg': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'db_sg': 'sg-1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9', 'status': 'Revoking old access' } print(f"Application Migration - Database Access Revocation") print(f"Old App SG: {migration_context['old_app_sg']}") print(f"New App SG: {migration_context['new_app_sg']}") print(f"") # Connection analysis connections_before = { 'old_app_connections': 250, 'new_app_connections': 750, 'total_connections': 1000, 'pool_limit': 1000 } print(f"Database connection pool (before revoke):") print(f" Old app connections: {connections_before['old_app_connections']}") print(f" New app connections: {connections_before['new_app_connections']}") print(f" Total usage: {connections_before['total_connections']}/{connections_before['pool_limit']}") print(f"") # After revocation connections_after = { 'old_app_connections': 0, 'new_app_connections': 750, 'total_connections': 750, 'capacity_freed': 25 } print(f"Database connection pool (after revoke):") print(f" Old app connections: {connections_after['old_app_connections']} (freed)") print(f" New app connections: {connections_after['new_app_connections']}") print(f" Total usage: {connections_after['total_connections']}/{connections_before['pool_limit']}") print(f" Capacity freed: {connections_after['capacity_freed']}%") print(f"") # Migration timeline migration_steps = { 'step1': 'Deploy new app (Django 5.2.5)', 'step2': 'Authorize new SG for database access', 'step3': 'Migrate traffic gradually (ALB)', 'step4': 'Monitor error rates and performance', 'step5': 'Revoke old SG access (THIS STEP)', 'step6': 'Drain remaining connections (1 hour)', 'step7': 'Terminate old instances' } print(f"Migration timeline:") for step, action in migration_steps.items(): status = '✓' if step == 'step5' else '→' print(f" {status} {action}") # Django models still accessible models_access = { 'JobPost': 'Read/write enabled', 'Author': 'Read/write enabled', 'Skills': 'Read/write enabled', 'Location': 'Read/write enabled', 'User': 'Read/write enabled' } print(f"\nModels accessible to new app:") for model, access in models_access.items(): print(f" ✓ {model}: {access}") print(f"\n✓ Old app database access revoked") print(f"✓ Capacity freed for new application")
Example 4: Revoke Temporary Development Access

Remove temporary SSH access from developer IP address after debugging and troubleshooting is complete.

#!/bin/bash
# Revoke temporary SSH access from developer
DEV_SG="sg-4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k"
DEVELOPER_IP="203.0.113.50"  # Developer's static IP
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Revoking temporary SSH access..."
echo "Developer IP: $DEVELOPER_IP"
echo ""

# Remove temporary SSH rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $DEV_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 22 \
  --cidr "$DEVELOPER_IP/32" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Temporary SSH rule revoked"
echo ""
echo "Access revoked:"
echo "  Developer SSH from $DEVELOPER_IP: DENIED"
echo "  Regular VPN access: Still available"
echo ""
echo "For future debugging:"
echo "  Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager"
echo "  Or: Add temporary rule with --description"
Revoking temporary SSH access... Developer IP: 203.0.113.50 { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoked-dev-ssh", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 22, "ToPort": 22, "CidrIpv4": "203.0.113.50/32", "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ Temporary SSH rule revoked
# Revoke temporary development SSH access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime, timedelta dev_access = { 'developer_ip': '203.0.113.50', 'cidr': '203.0.113.50/32', 'purpose': 'Temporary debugging', 'access_started': '2026-01-09 14:30 UTC', 'access_ended': '2026-01-11 16:45 UTC' } print(f"Development SSH Access - Temporary Revocation") print(f"Developer IP: {dev_access['developer_ip']}") print(f"Duration: 2 days, 2 hours") print(f"") # Access history access_history = { 'login_attempts': 12, 'successful_logins': 11, 'failed_logins': 1, 'average_session_mins': 45 } print(f"SSH Access History:") print(f" Login attempts: {access_history['login_attempts']}") print(f" Successful: {access_history['successful_logins']}") print(f" Failed: {access_history['failed_logins']}") print(f" Avg session: {access_history['average_session_mins']} min") print(f"") # Work completed during access work_completed = { 'issues_debugged': 3, 'logs_analyzed': 5, 'configs_reviewed': 2, 'databases_queried': 10 } print(f"Work completed during access:") for task, count in work_completed.items(): print(f" - {task}: {count}") print(f"") # Cleanup performed cleanup = { 'temp_files': 'Deleted', 'debug_logs': 'Collected', 'database_changes': 'None', 'findings': 'Documented in Jira' } print(f"Cleanup status:") for item, status in cleanup.items(): print(f" ✓ {item}: {status}") # Alternative access methods alternatives = { 'ssm_session_manager': 'Preferred - No SSH needed', 'bastion_host': 'Jump through VPN bastion', 'aws_systems_manager': 'Systems Manager Session Manager', 'cloudshell': 'AWS CloudShell in console' } print(f"\nAlternative access methods for future debugging:") for method, description in alternatives.items(): print(f" - {description}") # Security audit security_notes = { 'unauthorized_access': 'None detected', 'data_exposure': 'No sensitive data accessed', 'compliance': 'Temporary access compliant', 'next_review': '2026-01-18' } print(f"\nSecurity audit:") for check, result in security_notes.items(): print(f" ✓ {check}: {result}") print(f"\n✓ Temporary SSH access revoked") print(f"✓ Principle of least privilege maintained")
Example 5: Revoke Access After Security Incident

Quickly revoke access from a security group as part of incident response when suspicious activity is detected.

#!/bin/bash
# Incident response: Revoke access after suspicious activity
AFFECTED_SG="sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0"
ATTACKER_CIDR="198.51.100.0/24"  # Suspicious source
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "⚠ SECURITY INCIDENT RESPONSE"
echo "Revoking access from suspicious source..."
echo "Source: $ATTACKER_CIDR"
echo ""

# Revoke all TCP traffic from suspicious source
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $AFFECTED_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 0-65535 \
  --cidr $ATTACKER_CIDR \
  --region $REGION

# Alternative: revoke all protocols
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id $AFFECTED_SG \
  --protocol -1 \
  --cidr $ATTACKER_CIDR \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Suspicious traffic blocked"
echo ""
echo "Incident response actions:"
echo "  1. ✓ Access revoked"
echo "  2. → Enable WAF rules"
echo "  3. → Review CloudTrail logs"
echo "  4. → Notify security team"
echo "  5. → Document incident"
⚠ SECURITY INCIDENT RESPONSE Revoking access from suspicious source... Source: 198.51.100.0/24 { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoked-incident", "IpProtocol": "-1", "FromPort": 0, "ToPort": 65535, "CidrIpv4": "198.51.100.0/24", "IsEgress": false }] } ✓ Suspicious traffic blocked Incident response actions: 1. ✓ Access revoked 2. → Enable WAF rules 3. → Review CloudTrail logs
# Revoke access - incident response from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime incident = { 'incident_id': 'INC-2026-0145', 'detected_at': '2026-01-11 14:22:15 UTC', 'source_cidr': '198.51.100.0/24', 'threat_level': 'HIGH' } print(f"Security Incident Response Log") print(f"Incident ID: {incident['incident_id']}") print(f"Threat level: {incident['threat_level']}") print(f"Detected: {incident['detected_at']}") print(f"") # Attack timeline attack_timeline = { '14:15:30': 'First suspicious request detected', '14:16:45': 'Multiple failed auth attempts (10)', '14:18:20': 'SQL injection patterns detected', '14:19:00': 'Rate limiting triggered (100 req/sec)', '14:22:15': 'Alert escalated to security team', '14:23:00': 'Access revoked via SG rule' } print(f"Attack timeline:") for time, event in attack_timeline.items(): print(f" {time}: {event}") print(f"") # Blocked attacks blocked_attacks = { 'sql_injection': 'Detected and blocked', 'brute_force': 'Failed login attempts blocked', 'ddos': 'Rate limiting applied', 'port_scanning': 'Port scans detected' } print(f"Attacks detected and blocked:") for attack_type, status in blocked_attacks.items(): print(f" ✓ {attack_type}: {status}") # Affected resources affected_resources = { 'api_endpoints': 5, 'database_connections': 0, 'file_uploads': 0, 'successful_breaches': 0 } print(f"\nAffected resources:") for resource, count in affected_resources.items(): print(f" {resource}: {count}") # Response actions taken response_actions = { 'revoke_access': '✓ Completed at 14:23:00', 'enable_waf': '→ In progress', 'cloudtrail_review': '→ Queued', 'incident_ticket': '→ INC-2026-0145 created', 'customer_notification': '→ Planned' } print(f"\nIncident response actions:") for action, status in response_actions.items(): print(f" {status}") # Security measures active security_measures = { 'access_revoked': True, 'waf_enabled': False, 'extra_logging': True, 'team_alerted': True } print(f"\nActive security measures:") for measure, enabled in security_measures.items(): status = '✓' if enabled else '→' print(f" {status} {measure}") print(f"\n✓ Incident response completed") print(f"✓ Threat source blocked")

revoke-security-group-egress

Remove outbound rules from a security group. Used to revoke network access that was previously granted for outbound traffic. Useful for implementing zero-trust networking and restricting external communication.

Syntax
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id  \
  --protocol  \
  --port  \
  --cidr  | --destination-security-group-id  \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --group-id (string): The ID of the security group to revoke egress rules from. Format: sg-xxxxxxxx
  • --protocol (string): The protocol number or name (tcp, udp, icmp, or -1 for all). Use 'tcp' for HTTP/HTTPS/MySQL, 'udp' for DNS.
  • --port (integer): The port or port range to revoke (e.g., 80, 443, 3306, 6379). Omit for protocols that don't use ports.
  • --cidr (string): The CIDR block to revoke outbound access to (e.g., 0.0.0.0/0 for internet, 10.0.0.0/8 for VPC).
  • --destination-security-group-id (string): Another security group ID instead of CIDR. Revokes traffic to that group.
  • --region (string): AWS region where the security group is located (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.)
Return Value
  • Return (boolean): True if the rule was successfully revoked, false otherwise
  • UnknownIpPermissions (Array): Rules that couldn't be found to revoke (may indicate rule already removed)
  • SecurityGroupRuleId (String): ID of the revoked rule for tracking purposes
Example 1: Revoke Public Internet Access for Zero-Trust Network

Remove all outbound access to 0.0.0.0/0 to implement zero-trust networking where only specific internal resources are accessible.

#!/bin/bash
# Implement zero-trust: revoke default allow-all outbound rule
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Implementing zero-trust network security..."
echo "Revoking default allow-all outbound rule..."
echo ""

# Remove default allow-all egress rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol -1 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Default allow-all rule revoked"
echo ""
echo "Outbound restrictions activated:"
echo "  Public internet: DENIED"
echo "  Database (10.0.2.0/24): Available via specific rules"
echo "  Cache (10.0.3.0/24): Available via specific rules"
echo "  DNS (port 53): Available via specific rules"
echo ""
echo "Next: Add specific authorize-security-group-egress rules"
Implementing zero-trust network security... Revoking default allow-all outbound rule... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoke-default", "IpProtocol": "-1", "FromPort": 0, "ToPort": 65535, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ Default allow-all rule revoked
# Revoke public internet access - zero-trust from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone zerotrust_config = { 'group_id': 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8', 'old_policy': 'Allow all outbound (0.0.0.0/0)', 'new_policy': 'Deny all by default, allow specific', 'security_posture': 'ENHANCED' } print(f"Zero-Trust Network Implementation") print(f"Security Group: {zerotrust_config['group_id']}") print(f"") # Outbound traffic before revocation before_traffic = { 'internet_access': True, 'external_apis': True, 'public_dns': True, 'unauthorized_destinations': 'POSSIBLE' } print(f"Before revocation (default allow-all):") for destination, allowed in before_traffic.items(): status = '✓ ALLOWED' if allowed else '✗ DENIED' print(f" {destination}: {status}") print(f"") # After revocation after_traffic = { 'internet_access': False, 'internal_database': True, 'internal_cache': True, 'vpc_dns': True, 'unauthorized_destinations': 'DENIED' } print(f"After revocation (zero-trust):") for destination, allowed in after_traffic.items(): status = '✓ ALLOWED' if allowed else '✗ DENIED' print(f" {destination}: {status}") print(f"") # VPC network topology network_layers = { 'application': '10.0.1.0/24', 'database': '10.0.2.0/24', 'cache': '10.0.3.0/24' } print(f"VPC topology (all outbound restricted to):") for layer, cidr in network_layers.items(): print(f" - {layer}: {cidr}") # Data exfiltration prevention security_benefits = { 'data_exfiltration': 'Prevented - no internet access', 'malware_callbacks': 'Blocked - no external connectivity', 'unauthorized_apis': 'Blocked - not in approved list', 'compliance': 'PCI-DSS, HIPAA compatible' } print(f"\nSecurity benefits:") for benefit, detail in security_benefits.items(): print(f" ✓ {benefit}: {detail}") # Transition impact transition = { 'django_instances': 'Still function (internal resources only)', 'external_apis': 'Will fail unless whitelisted', 'package_downloads': 'Will fail (need NAT gateway)', 'dns_resolution': 'Works (internal VPC DNS)' } print(f"\nApplication impact:") for component, impact in transition.items(): print(f" {component}: {impact}") print(f"\n✓ Zero-trust networking activated") print(f"✓ Outbound default-deny policy enabled")
Example 2: Revoke External API Access During Incident

Remove outbound HTTPS access to external APIs as part of incident containment to prevent data exfiltration.

#!/bin/bash
# Incident response: Block external API calls
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "⚠ INCIDENT CONTAINMENT"
echo "Revoking external API access..."
echo ""

# Revoke HTTPS to external APIs (port 443)
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 443 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ External HTTPS access revoked"
echo ""
echo "API access blocked:"
echo "  Stripe: BLOCKED"
echo "  SendGrid: BLOCKED"
echo "  OAuth providers: BLOCKED"
echo "  Third-party services: BLOCKED"
echo ""
echo "Internal services (still available):"
echo "  Database (RDS): AVAILABLE"
echo "  Cache (Redis): AVAILABLE"
echo "  DNS (Route53): AVAILABLE"
⚠ INCIDENT CONTAINMENT Revoking external API access... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoke-https-api", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "CidrIpv4": "0.0.0.0/0", "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ External HTTPS access revoked
# Revoke external API access - incident response from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime incident_response = { 'incident_id': 'INC-2026-0147', 'detected_at': '2026-01-11 15:45 UTC', 'action': 'Revoke external API access', 'severity': 'CRITICAL' } print(f"Incident Response - API Access Revocation") print(f"Incident ID: {incident_response['incident_id']}") print(f"Severity: {incident_response['severity']}") print(f"Time: {incident_response['detected_at']}") print(f"") # External APIs affected external_apis = { 'stripe': { 'status': 'REVOKED', 'function': 'Payment processing', 'impact': 'Cannot process payments' }, 'sendgrid': { 'status': 'REVOKED', 'function': 'Email sending', 'impact': 'Cannot send emails' }, 'oauth': { 'status': 'REVOKED', 'function': 'Social login', 'impact': 'Cannot authenticate via OAuth' } } print(f"Blocked external APIs:") for api, config in external_apis.items(): print(f" {api}:") print(f" Status: {config['status']}") print(f" Impact: {config['impact']}") print(f"") # Application functionality analysis functionality = { 'user_authentication': 'Partial (local only)', 'payment_processing': 'BLOCKED', 'email_notifications': 'BLOCKED', 'data_access': 'Available (internal)', 'database_operations': 'Available' } print(f"Application functionality:") for feature, status in functionality.items(): print(f" {feature}: {status}") print(f"") # Data integrity checks integrity = { 'exfiltration_risk': 'ELIMINATED', 'api_abuse': 'BLOCKED', 'malicious_calls': 'BLOCKED', 'customer_data': 'PROTECTED' } print(f"Data security status:") for check, result in integrity.items(): print(f" ✓ {check}: {result}") # Containment timeline timeline = { '15:45': 'Incident detected', '15:47': 'Investigation started', '15:50': 'API access revoked', '15:52': 'External calls blocked', '16:00': 'Customer notification sent' } print(f"\nIncident timeline:") for time, event in timeline.items(): print(f" {time}: {event}") # Recovery steps recovery = { 'step1': 'Analyze logs for unauthorized API calls', 'step2': 'Identify compromised credentials', 'step3': 'Reset API keys/tokens', 'step4': 'Review and re-authorize API calls', 'step5': 'Restore external API access' } print(f"\nRecovery steps (in progress):") for step, action in recovery.items(): print(f" {step}: {action}") print(f"\n✓ External API access contained") print(f"✓ Incident response in progress")
Example 3: Revoke Cache Access When Decommissioning Redis

Remove outbound access to Redis cache when migrating to a different caching solution or decommissioning the cache.

#!/bin/bash
# Decommission Redis: revoke cache access
APP_SG="sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
OLD_CACHE_SG="sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Decommissioning Redis cache..."
echo "Revoking outbound Redis access..."
echo ""

# Revoke Redis port 6379 access
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 6379 \
  --destination-security-group-id $OLD_CACHE_SG \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Redis access revoked"
echo ""
echo "Cache decommissioning:"
echo "  Redis (6379): BLOCKED"
echo "  Session storage: Using database instead"
echo "  Query cache: Using new cache solution"
echo ""
echo "Migration status: In progress"
echo "  Phase 1: ✓ New cache deployed"
echo "  Phase 2: ✓ Gradual traffic migration"
echo "  Phase 3: → Revoke old cache access (THIS STEP)"
echo "  Phase 4: → Monitor application"
echo "  Phase 5: → Decommission Redis cluster"
Decommissioning Redis cache... Revoking outbound Redis access... { "Return": true, "SecurityGroupRules": [{ "GroupId": "sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8", "SecurityGroupRuleId": "sgr-revoke-redis", "IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 6379, "ToPort": 6379, "ReferencedGroupInfo": { "GroupId": "sg-2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0" }, "IsEgress": true }] } ✓ Redis access revoked
# Revoke Redis cache access - decommissioning from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from django.core.cache import cache cache_migration = { 'old_cache': 'Redis (ElastiCache)', 'new_cache': 'Memcached', 'status': 'Migration in progress', 'phase': 3 } print(f"Cache Migration - Redis Decommissioning") print(f"Old cache: {cache_migration['old_cache']}") print(f"New cache: {cache_migration['new_cache']}") print(f"Phase: {cache_migration['phase']} of 5") print(f"") # Cache usage before revocation cache_usage_before = { 'sessions': 'Redis', 'query_cache': 'Redis', 'user_cache': 'Redis', 'temp_data': 'Redis', 'cache_size_mb': 2048 } print(f"Cache configuration (before revoke):") for usage, location in cache_usage_before.items(): print(f" {usage}: {location}") print(f"") # Cache usage after revocation cache_usage_after = { 'sessions': 'Database (PostgreSQL)', 'query_cache': 'Memcached', 'user_cache': 'Memcached', 'temp_data': 'Memcached', 'cache_size_mb': 1024 } print(f"Cache configuration (after revoke):") for usage, location in cache_usage_after.items(): print(f" {usage}: {location}") print(f"") # Performance impact analysis performance = { 'session_lookup_ms': 15, 'cache_hit_rate': 78, 'queries_per_second': 150, 'avg_response_ms': 250 } print(f"Expected performance metrics:") print(f" Session lookup: {performance['session_lookup_ms']}ms") print(f" Cache hit rate: {performance['cache_hit_rate']}%") print(f" Throughput: {performance['queries_per_second']} q/s") print(f" Response time: {performance['avg_response_ms']}ms") print(f"") # Data migration checklist migration_checklist = { 'new_cache_deployed': True, 'cache_warmed': True, 'traffic_migrated': 80, 'old_cache_drained': False, 'monitoring_active': True } print(f"Migration checklist:") for step, completed in migration_checklist.items(): status = '✓' if isinstance(completed, bool) and completed else f'{completed}%' if isinstance(completed, int) else '✗' print(f" {status} {step}") # Rollback plan rollback_available = { 'restore_redis': 'Yes (5 min)', 'restore_auth': 'Yes (immediate)', 'data_loss': 'None (cached data only)' } print(f"\nRollback capability:") for item, availability in rollback_available.items(): print(f" {item}: {availability}") print(f"\n✓ Redis access revoked") print(f"✓ Cache migration proceeding")
Example 4: Revoke NTP Access After Fixing Time Synchronization
