  --rule-name {rule-name} \
  --start-time {timestamp} \
  --end-time {timestamp} \
  --period {seconds}
Parameters Description
  • --rule-name (string, required): Name of the Contributor Insights rule
  • --start-time (timestamp, required): Start of time range
  • --end-time (timestamp, required): End of time range
  • --period (integer, required): Period in seconds
Return Value
  • Contributors (Array): Top contributors identified by the rule
  • MetricDatapoints (Array): Aggregated metric values

enable-insight-rules

Enables specified Contributor Insights rules.

Syntax
aws cloudwatch enable-insight-rules \
  --rule-names {rule-name1} {rule-name2}
Parameters Description
  • --rule-names (list, required): Names of rules to enable
Return Value
  • Failures (Array): List of rules that failed to enable (if any)

disable-insight-rules

Disables specified Contributor Insights rules.

Syntax
aws cloudwatch disable-insight-rules \
  --rule-names {rule-name1} {rule-name2}
Parameters Description
  • --rule-names (list, required): Names of rules to disable
Return Value
  • Failures (Array): List of rules that failed to disable (if any)

delete-insight-rules

Deletes specified Contributor Insights rules permanently.

Syntax
aws cloudwatch delete-insight-rules \
  --rule-names {rule-name1} {rule-name2}
Parameters Description
  • --rule-names (list, required): Names of rules to delete
Return Value
  • Failures (Array): List of rules that failed to delete (if any)

list-tags-for-resource

Lists tags for a CloudWatch resource (alarm, insight rule, etc.).

Syntax
aws cloudwatch list-tags-for-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CloudWatch resource
Return Value
  • Tags (Array): List of tags with Key and Value

tag-resource

Adds tags to a CloudWatch resource for organization and cost allocation.

Syntax
aws cloudwatch tag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tags Key={key},Value={value}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CloudWatch resource
  • --tags (list, required): List of tags to add
Return Value
  • ResponseMetadata (Object): HTTP 200 on success

untag-resource

Removes tags from a CloudWatch resource.

Syntax
aws cloudwatch untag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tag-keys {key1} {key2}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CloudWatch resource
  • --tag-keys (list, required): List of tag keys to remove
Return Value
  • ResponseMetadata (Object): HTTP 200 on success

🏗️ AWS CloudFormation

Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service for deploying and managing AWS resources declaratively

📋 CloudFormation Overview

AWS CloudFormation enables you to model, provision, and manage AWS resources using templates. Define your entire infrastructure as code, including EC2 instances for your anihpj Django application, RDS databases for jobpost data, S3 buckets for media storage, and networking components - all in JSON or YAML format.

Core CloudFormation Operations for Django Infrastructure

create-stack - Deploy new infrastructure
update-stack - Modify existing stacks
delete-stack - Remove infrastructure
describe-stacks - Get stack details
list-stacks - List all stacks
validate-template - Check template syntax
create-change-set - Preview changes
execute-change-set - Apply changes
deploy - High-level deployment
package - Package Lambda code
detect-stack-drift - Check drift
get-template - Retrieve template

create-stack

Creates a new CloudFormation stack from a template. This is the primary way to deploy your Django infrastructure including EC2, RDS, VPC, and all resources needed for the anihpj jobpost application.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Unique name for the stack. Example: anihpj-django-infrastructure
  • --template-body (string): Path to template file. Use file:// prefix for local files
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of template (for templates > 51,200 bytes)
  • --parameters (list): Input parameters as ParameterKey=key,ParameterValue=value pairs
  • --capabilities (list): Required for IAM resources. Values: CAPABILITY_IAM, CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM
  • --tags (list): Tags to apply. Format: Key=Environment,Value=production
  • --on-failure (string): Action on failure. Values: DO_NOTHING, ROLLBACK, DELETE
Return Value
  • StackId (string): Unique identifier for the created stack
5 Detailed CloudFormation create-stack Examples
Example 1: Create Django Application Stack with EC2 and RDS

Deploy complete Django infrastructure including EC2 instance, RDS PostgreSQL database, VPC, and security groups.

#!/bin/bash
# Create Django application infrastructure stack
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,ParameterValue=db.t3.micro \
    ParameterKey=KeyPairName,ParameterValue=anihpj-key \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Stack creation initiated"
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890" } ✓ Stack creation initiated Stack Status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS Resources being created: - VPC (anihpj-vpc) - Subnets (public, private) - Internet Gateway - Security Groups (django-sg, rds-sg) - EC2 Instance (t3.medium) - RDS PostgreSQL (db.t3.micro) Estimated completion: 10-15 minutes
# Django infrastructure template (django-infrastructure.yaml) AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Description: Complete Django infrastructure for anihpj jobpost application Parameters: InstanceType: Type: String Default: t3.medium AllowedValues: [t3.micro, t3.small, t3.medium] Description: EC2 instance type for Django application DBInstanceClass: Type: String Default: db.t3.micro Description: RDS instance class for PostgreSQL database KeyPairName: Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName Description: EC2 key pair for SSH access Resources: # VPC Configuration VPC: Type: AWS::EC2::VPC Properties: CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16 EnableDnsHostnames: true EnableDnsSupport: true Tags: - Key: Name Value: anihpj-vpc # Public Subnet for Django EC2 PublicSubnet: Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet Properties: VpcId: !Ref VPC CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24 AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs ''] MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true Tags: - Key: Name Value: anihpj-public-subnet # Security Group for Django application DjangoSecurityGroup: Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup Properties: GroupDescription: Allow HTTP, HTTPS, SSH for Django VpcId: !Ref VPC SecurityGroupIngress: - IpProtocol: tcp FromPort: 22 ToPort: 22 CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0 - IpProtocol: tcp FromPort: 80 ToPort: 80 CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0 - IpProtocol: tcp FromPort: 443 ToPort: 443 CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0 - IpProtocol: tcp FromPort: 8000 ToPort: 8000 CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0 # EC2 Instance for Django DjangoInstance: Type: AWS::EC2::Instance Properties: InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType ImageId: ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0 # Amazon Linux 2 KeyName: !Ref KeyPairName SecurityGroupIds: - !Ref DjangoSecurityGroup SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet UserData: Fn::Base64: !Sub | #!/bin/bash yum update -y yum install -y python3 python3-pip git docker pip3 install django gunicorn psycopg2-binary systemctl start docker systemctl enable docker Tags: - Key: Name Value: anihpj-django-server # RDS PostgreSQL Database JobPostDatabase: Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Properties: DBInstanceClass: !Ref DBInstanceClass Engine: postgres EngineVersion: '15.3' MasterUsername: anihpj_admin MasterUserPassword: '{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj-db-password}}' AllocatedStorage: 20 DBName: anihpj_jobpost VPCSecurityGroups: - !Ref RDSSecurityGroup Tags: - Key: Name Value: anihpj-jobpost-db Outputs: DjangoInstancePublicIP: Description: Public IP of Django EC2 instance Value: !GetAtt DjangoInstance.PublicIp DatabaseEndpoint: Description: RDS database endpoint Value: !GetAtt JobPostDatabase.Endpoint.Address
Example 2: Create VPC Stack with Multi-AZ Configuration

Deploy a production-ready VPC with public/private subnets across multiple availability zones for high availability.

#!/bin/bash
# Create multi-AZ VPC infrastructure
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-vpc-infrastructure \
  --template-body file://vpc-multi-az.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=VpcCIDR,ParameterValue=10.0.0.0/16 \
    ParameterKey=PublicSubnet1CIDR,ParameterValue=10.0.1.0/24 \
    ParameterKey=PublicSubnet2CIDR,ParameterValue=10.0.2.0/24 \
    ParameterKey=PrivateSubnet1CIDR,ParameterValue=10.0.11.0/24 \
    ParameterKey=PrivateSubnet2CIDR,ParameterValue=10.0.12.0/24 \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=ManagedBy,Value=CloudFormation \
  --region us-east-1
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-vpc-infrastructure/abcd1234" } Creating VPC resources: - VPC (10.0.0.0/16) - Internet Gateway - Public Subnets in us-east-1a, us-east-1b - Private Subnets in us-east-1a, us-east-1b - NAT Gateways (2) - Route Tables (4) Stack Status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS → CREATE_COMPLETE (5-7 minutes)
Example 3: Create S3 and CloudFront Stack for Django Static Files

Deploy S3 buckets for media/static files and CloudFront CDN for the anihpj jobpost application.

#!/bin/bash
# Create S3 and CloudFront stack for Django assets
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-storage-cdn \
  --template-body file://s3-cloudfront.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=BucketNamePrefix,ParameterValue=anihpj-jobpost \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --tags Key=Application,Value=Django Key=Component,Value=Storage \
  --region us-east-1

# Wait for completion
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-storage-cdn
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-storage-cdn/5678efgh" } Creating storage resources: ✓ S3 Bucket: anihpj-jobpost-media-prod ✓ S3 Bucket: anihpj-jobpost-static-prod ✓ CloudFront Distribution (for static assets) ✓ Origin Access Identity ✓ Bucket Policies (read access for CloudFront) Stack Status: CREATE_COMPLETE CloudFront URL: d1234abcd.cloudfront.net
# Django settings.py configuration after stack creation # Retrieve outputs from CloudFormation import boto3 cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation') response = cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName='anihpj-storage-cdn') outputs = {o['OutputKey']: o['OutputValue'] for o in response['Stacks'][0]['Outputs']} # Update Django settings AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = outputs['MediaBucketName'] # anihpj-jobpost-media-prod AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = outputs['CloudFrontDomain'] # d1234abcd.cloudfront.net STATIC_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/static/' MEDIA_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/media/' # JobPost model using CloudFormation-provisioned storage from django.db import models class JobPost(models.Model): title = models.CharField(max_length=200) company_logo = models.ImageField(upload_to='logos/') # → S3/CloudFront description = models.TextField() def save(self, *args, **kwargs): # Files automatically uploaded to S3 bucket from CloudFormation stack super().save(*args, **kwargs)
Example 4: Create Auto Scaling Group Stack for Django

Deploy Auto Scaling Group with Launch Template for automatic scaling of Django application servers.

#!/bin/bash
# Create Auto Scaling stack for Django application
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-autoscaling \
  --template-body file://asg-template.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=MinSize,ParameterValue=2 \
    ParameterKey=MaxSize,ParameterValue=10 \
    ParameterKey=DesiredCapacity,ParameterValue=3 \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
    ParameterKey=AMIId,ParameterValue=ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0 \
    ParameterKey=VpcId,ParameterValue=vpc-0123456789abcdef0 \
    ParameterKey=SubnetIds,ParameterValue="subnet-111\\,subnet-222" \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --tags Key=Scaling,Value=Auto Key=Application,Value=Django
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-autoscaling/9abc0def" } Creating Auto Scaling resources: ✓ Launch Template (Django app configuration) ✓ Target Group (health checks on /health/) ✓ Application Load Balancer ✓ Auto Scaling Group (min=2, desired=3, max=10) ✓ Scaling Policies (CPU-based, 70% threshold) ✓ CloudWatch Alarms Current Instances: 3 (healthy) Load Balancer DNS: anihpj-alb-1234567890.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com
# Django health check view for ALB target group from django.http import JsonResponse from django.db import connection def health_check(request): """Health endpoint for CloudFormation ALB target group""" try: # Check database connectivity with connection.cursor() as cursor: cursor.execute("SELECT 1") return JsonResponse({ 'status': 'healthy', 'database': 'connected', 'stack': 'anihpj-django-autoscaling' }) except Exception as e: return JsonResponse({ 'status': 'unhealthy', 'error': str(e) }, status=503) # urls.py from django.urls import path from . import views urlpatterns = [ path('health/', views.health_check, name='health_check'), # ALB target group uses this endpoint ]
Example 5: Create Nested Stack with Cross-Stack References

Deploy master stack that references other stacks using exports and imports for modular infrastructure.

#!/bin/bash
# Create master stack that orchestrates multiple nested stacks
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-master-stack \
  --template-body file://master-stack.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production \
    ParameterKey=ProjectName,ParameterValue=anihpj \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND \
  --tags Key=StackType,Value=Master Key=Project,Value=anihpj \
  --region us-east-1

# Monitor nested stack creation
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-master-stack \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceType==`AWS::CloudFormation::Stack`].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatus]' \
  --output table
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-master-stack/master-123" } Creating nested stacks: 1. NetworkStack (VPC, Subnets, Gateways) - CREATE_COMPLETE 2. SecurityStack (Security Groups, IAM Roles) - CREATE_COMPLETE 3. DatabaseStack (RDS PostgreSQL) - CREATE_IN_PROGRESS 4. StorageStack (S3, CloudFront) - PENDING 5. ComputeStack (EC2, Auto Scaling) - PENDING Progress: 2/5 stacks complete Estimated completion: 15-20 minutes
# Master stack template with nested stacks (master-stack.yaml) AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Description: Master stack for anihpj Django application Parameters: Environment: Type: String Default: production ProjectName: Type: String Default: anihpj Resources: # Network Stack NetworkStack: Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack Properties: TemplateURL: https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-templates/network.yaml Parameters: Environment: !Ref Environment Tags: - Key: Component Value: Network # Database Stack (depends on Network) DatabaseStack: Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack DependsOn: NetworkStack Properties: TemplateURL: https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-templates/database.yaml Parameters: VpcId: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.VpcId PrivateSubnet1: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.PrivateSubnet1Id PrivateSubnet2: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.PrivateSubnet2Id # Compute Stack (depends on Database) ComputeStack: Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack DependsOn: DatabaseStack Properties: TemplateURL: https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-templates/compute.yaml Parameters: DatabaseEndpoint: !GetAtt DatabaseStack.Outputs.DBEndpoint VpcId: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.VpcId Outputs: MasterStackId: Value: !Ref AWS::StackId ApplicationURL: Value: !GetAtt ComputeStack.Outputs.LoadBalancerURL

update-stack

Updates an existing CloudFormation stack with a new template or parameter values. Use this to modify your Django infrastructure without recreating resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the stack to update
  • --template-body (string): Path to new template file
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of new template
  • --use-previous-template: Reuse existing template, only update parameters
  • --parameters (list): New parameter values or UsePreviousValue=true
  • --capabilities (list): IAM capabilities if template creates IAM resources
Return Value
  • StackId (string): Unique identifier of the updated stack
5 Detailed CloudFormation update-stack Examples
Example 1: Update EC2 Instance Type for Django Application

Scale up Django EC2 instances from t3.medium to t3.large for better performance.

#!/bin/bash
# Update instance type parameter only
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=KeyPairName,UsePreviousValue=true \
  --region us-east-1

# Wait for update to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "✓ Stack updated successfully"
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d" } Update initiated - UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS Resources being updated: - DjangoInstance (t3.medium → t3.large) Status: Requires replacement (will create new instance) Update plan: 1. Create new EC2 instance (t3.large) 2. Update DNS/Load Balancer 3. Terminate old instance (t3.medium) ✓ Stack updated successfully New instance ready: i-0abcd1234ef567890
Example 2: Add CloudWatch Alarms to Existing Stack

Update template to include monitoring alarms for CPU, memory, and database connections.

#!/bin/bash
# Update stack with new monitoring resources
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure-with-monitoring.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=KeyPairName,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=AlarmEmail,ParameterValue=admin@anihpj.com \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d" } Adding new resources: ✓ SNS Topic (anihpj-alarms) ✓ SNS Subscription (admin@anihpj.com) ✓ CloudWatch Alarm: High CPU Usage (>80%) ✓ CloudWatch Alarm: Low Disk Space (<20%) ✓ CloudWatch Alarm: Database Connection Count (>100) ✓ CloudWatch Dashboard No resource replacement required Update Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE
# Django middleware to track metrics for CloudWatch alarms from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin import boto3 import time class CloudWatchMetricsMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin): def __init__(self, get_response): super().__init__(get_response) self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch') def process_request(self, request): request._start_time = time.time() def process_response(self, request, response): # Track request duration duration = time.time() - request._start_time # Send to CloudWatch (triggers alarms if thresholds exceeded) self.cloudwatch.put_metric_data( Namespace='AnihpjDjango', MetricData=[ { 'MetricName': 'RequestDuration', 'Value': duration, 'Unit': 'Seconds' }, { 'MetricName': 'StatusCode', 'Value': response.status_code, 'Unit': 'Count' } ] ) return response
Example 3: Update RDS Database Configuration

Increase database storage and enable Multi-AZ for production workload.

#!/bin/bash
# Update RDS configuration for better performance
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=KeyPairName,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=DBAllocatedStorage,ParameterValue=100 \
    ParameterKey=MultiAZ,ParameterValue=true \
  --region us-east-1

# Monitor update progress
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 10
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d" } Updating RDS configuration: - Allocated Storage: 20 GB → 100 GB - Multi-AZ: false → true Update process: 1. Taking automated snapshot... ✓ 2. Modifying primary instance... ✓ 3. Creating standby replica in us-east-1b... (in progress) 4. Enabling synchronous replication... Estimated completion: 15-20 minutes No downtime expected (online modification)
# Django database configuration after RDS update # settings.py - Automatically uses Multi-AZ endpoint DATABASES = { 'default': { 'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql', 'NAME': 'anihpj_jobpost', 'USER': 'anihpj_admin', 'PASSWORD': os.environ['DB_PASSWORD'], # Multi-AZ endpoint (CloudFormation output) 'HOST': 'anihpj-db.c9abc12def34.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com', 'PORT': '5432', 'OPTIONS': { 'connect_timeout': 10, } } } # Automatic failover handling # If primary AZ fails, RDS automatically promotes standby # Django reconnects transparently (typically 1-2 minutes downtime)
Example 4: Update Security Groups to Add New Rules

Modify security group rules to allow traffic from new IP ranges or services.

#!/bin/bash
# Update stack to add new security group rules
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure-updated-sg.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=KeyPairName,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=AllowedSSHCIDR,ParameterValue=203.0.113.0/24 \
  --region us-east-1
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d" } Updating security groups: ✓ DjangoSecurityGroup - Added ingress rule: SSH from 203.0.113.0/24 - Added ingress rule: HTTPS from 0.0.0.0/0 - Removed old rule: HTTP from 0.0.0.0/0 ✓ RDSSecurityGroup - Modified: PostgreSQL access only from DjangoSecurityGroup Update Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE (30 seconds) No instance replacement required
Example 5: Rolling Update for Auto Scaling Group

Update AMI ID for Auto Scaling Group with rolling update policy to avoid downtime.

#!/bin/bash
# Update ASG with new AMI (zero-downtime rolling update)
NEW_AMI="ami-0abcdef1234567890"  # New Django application image

aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-autoscaling \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=AMIId,ParameterValue=$NEW_AMI \
    ParameterKey=MinSize,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=MaxSize,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=DesiredCapacity,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,UsePreviousValue=true \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

# Monitor rolling update
watch -n 10 'aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups \
  --auto-scaling-group-names anihpj-django-asg \
  --query "AutoScalingGroups[0].Instances[*].[InstanceId,HealthStatus,LifecycleState]" \
  --output table'
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-autoscaling/9abc0def" } Rolling update in progress: Current instances: 3 (old AMI) Target instances: 3 (new AMI) Update strategy: WaitOnResourceSignals Batch size: 1 instance at a time Pause time: 5 minutes between batches Progress: [=========> ] 33% (1/3 instances updated) - i-old1: Draining → Terminating → Terminated ✓ - i-new1: Launching → InService → Healthy ✓ - i-old2: InService (pending update) - i-old3: InService (pending update) Application availability: 100% maintained
# UserData script for new AMI (signals CloudFormation when ready) #!/bin/bash # Install and configure Django application yum update -y yum install -y python3 python3-pip aws-cfn-bootstrap # Install Django app pip3 install django gunicorn psycopg2-binary boto3 # Start application cd /opt/anihpj-django gunicorn --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 anihpj.wsgi:application --daemon # Wait for health check sleep 30 # Signal CloudFormation that instance is ready /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal -e $? \ --stack anihpj-django-autoscaling \ --resource AutoScalingGroup \ --region us-east-1 # CloudFormation waits for signal before proceeding to next instance

delete-stack

Deletes a CloudFormation stack and all its resources. Use with caution as this permanently removes infrastructure.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the stack to delete
  • --retain-resources (list): Logical IDs of resources to retain instead of deleting
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role ARN for CloudFormation to assume
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
5 Detailed CloudFormation delete-stack Examples
Example 1: Delete Development Stack Safely

Remove development environment stack after testing is complete.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete development stack with confirmation
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-dev"

# Check stack status before deletion
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].[StackName,StackStatus]' \
  --output table

# Delete the stack
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

echo "Deletion initiated for $STACK_NAME"

# Wait for deletion to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-delete-complete --stack-name $STACK_NAME

echo "✓ Stack deleted successfully"
-------------------------------------------- | DescribeStacks | +------------------------+-----------------+ | anihpj-django-dev | CREATE_COMPLETE| +------------------------+-----------------+ Deletion initiated for anihpj-django-dev Deleting resources in reverse order: ✓ EC2 Instance (i-0abcd1234) - terminated ✓ RDS Database (anihpj-dev-db) - deleted (snapshot: final-snapshot-2026-01-17) ✓ Security Groups - deleted ✓ Subnets - deleted ✓ Internet Gateway - detached and deleted ✓ VPC - deleted ✓ Stack deleted successfully Total deletion time: 8 minutes
Example 2: Delete Stack While Retaining S3 Bucket

Remove stack but keep S3 bucket with uploaded jobpost data for backup.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete stack but retain S3 bucket with important data
aws cloudformation delete-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-storage-cdn \
  --retain-resources MediaBucket

# The MediaBucket resource will NOT be deleted
# All other resources (CloudFront, policies, etc.) will be deleted

echo "Stack deletion in progress, MediaBucket retained"
Stack deletion in progress, MediaBucket retained Resources being deleted: ✓ CloudFront Distribution - disabled and deleted ✓ Origin Access Identity - deleted ✓ Bucket Policies - deleted ⊗ S3 Bucket (MediaBucket) - RETAINED └─ Bucket: anihpj-jobpost-media-prod (preserved) └─ Contents: 1,547 objects, 2.3 GB retained Stack Status: DELETE_COMPLETE Retained resource ARN: arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-jobpost-media-prod
# Django code to handle retained S3 bucket after stack deletion from django.conf import settings import boto3 # S3 bucket was retained from deleted CloudFormation stack # Update settings to use the existing bucket AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = 'anihpj-jobpost-media-prod' # Retained bucket s3_client = boto3.client('s3') # Verify bucket still exists after stack deletion try: s3_client.head_bucket(Bucket=AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME) print(f"✓ Retained bucket '{AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME}' is accessible") # List existing media files response = s3_client.list_objects_v2(Bucket=AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME, MaxKeys=10) print(f"✓ Found {response.get('KeyCount', 0)} objects in retained bucket") except Exception as e: print(f"✗ Error accessing retained bucket: {e}")
Example 3: Force Delete Stack with DeletionPolicy

Delete stack even when resources have DeletionPolicy set to Retain.

#!/bin/bash
# Some resources may have DeletionPolicy: Retain in template
# This creates final snapshots before deletion

aws cloudformation delete-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --region us-east-1

# Monitor deletion events
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceStatus==`DELETE_IN_PROGRESS` || ResourceStatus==`DELETE_COMPLETE`].[Timestamp,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatus]' \
  --output table
Deleting stack with DeletionPolicy considerations: Resources with DeletionPolicy: Snapshot ✓ RDS Database: Final snapshot created Snapshot: anihpj-db-final-snapshot-20260117 Resources with DeletionPolicy: Retain ⊗ S3 Bucket: anihpj-logs (RETAINED) ⊗ CloudWatch Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj (RETAINED) Resources with DeletionPolicy: Delete (default) ✓ EC2 Instances: Terminated ✓ Load Balancer: Deleted ✓ Auto Scaling Group: Deleted Stack Status: DELETE_COMPLETE
# Template showing DeletionPolicy configurations Resources: # Database with automatic snapshot on deletion JobPostDatabase: Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance DeletionPolicy: Snapshot # Creates final snapshot Properties: DBInstanceClass: db.t3.micro Engine: postgres # ... other properties # Logs retained for compliance ApplicationLogs: Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup DeletionPolicy: Retain # Keeps logs after stack deletion Properties: LogGroupName: /aws/django/anihpj RetentionInDays: 30 # Temporary compute deleted immediately DjangoInstance: Type: AWS::EC2::Instance DeletionPolicy: Delete # Default behavior Properties: InstanceType: t3.medium # ... other properties
Example 4: Delete All Stacks in Environment with Tag

Clean up entire environment by deleting all stacks with specific tag.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete all development environment stacks
ENVIRONMENT="development"

# List all stacks with Environment=development tag
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[*].StackName' \
  --output text)

for STACK in $STACKS; do
  # Check if stack has development tag
  TAGS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'Stacks[0].Tags[?Key==`Environment`].Value' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$TAGS" == "$ENVIRONMENT" ]; then
    echo "Deleting stack: $STACK"
    aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name $STACK
  fi
done

echo "All development stacks deleted"
Found 5 stacks with Environment=development tag: Deleting stack: anihpj-django-dev Deleting stack: anihpj-vpc-dev Deleting stack: anihpj-storage-dev Deleting stack: anihpj-database-dev Deleting stack: anihpj-monitoring-dev Deletion progress: [1/5] anihpj-monitoring-dev: DELETE_IN_PROGRESS → DELETE_COMPLETE ✓ [2/5] anihpj-django-dev: DELETE_IN_PROGRESS (waiting for dependencies) [3/5] anihpj-storage-dev: DELETE_IN_PROGRESS → DELETE_COMPLETE ✓ [4/5] anihpj-database-dev: DELETE_IN_PROGRESS [5/5] anihpj-vpc-dev: DELETE_PENDING (waiting for other stacks) All development stacks deleted Total resources removed: 47 Cost savings: ~$150/month
Example 5: Delete Stack with Failure Handling

Handle deletion failures and retry with resource retention if needed.

#!/bin/bash
# Attempt to delete stack with error handling
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

delete_stack() {
  aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME
  
  # Wait and check for errors
  aws cloudformation wait stack-delete-complete --stack-name $STACK_NAME 2>&1
  
  if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "⚠ Deletion failed, checking for stuck resources"
    
    # Find resources that failed to delete
    FAILED_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'StackResources[?ResourceStatus==`DELETE_FAILED`].LogicalResourceId' \
      --output text)
    
    if [ -n "$FAILED_RESOURCES" ]; then
      echo "Failed resources: $FAILED_RESOURCES"
      echo "Retrying with resource retention..."
      
      # Retry deletion, retaining problematic resources
      aws cloudformation delete-stack \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
        --retain-resources $FAILED_RESOURCES
    fi
  fi
}

delete_stack
Initial deletion attempt... ⚠ Deletion failed, checking for stuck resources Failed resources: NetworkInterface ENI-123456 Error details: - NetworkInterface (ENI-123456): Network interface is in use by another resource Cannot be deleted while attached Retrying with resource retention... Second deletion attempt: ✓ EC2 Instance - terminated ✓ Security Groups - deleted ✓ Load Balancer - deleted ⊗ NetworkInterface (ENI-123456) - RETAINED (manual cleanup required) ✓ Subnets - deleted ✓ VPC - deleted Stack Status: DELETE_COMPLETE (with 1 retained resource) Manual cleanup required: aws ec2 delete-network-interface --network-interface-id ENI-123456
# Python script for safe stack deletion with rollback import boto3 import time cf = boto3.client('cloudformation') def safe_delete_stack(stack_name, max_retries=3): """Safely delete CloudFormation stack with retry logic""" for attempt in range(max_retries): try: print(f"Deletion attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries}") # Initiate deletion cf.delete_stack(StackName=stack_name) # Wait for completion waiter = cf.get_waiter('stack_delete_complete') waiter.wait(StackName=stack_name) print(f"✓ Stack {stack_name} deleted successfully") return True except Exception as e: print(f"✗ Deletion failed: {str(e)}") if attempt < max_retries - 1: # Check for failed resources response = cf.describe_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) failed = [r['LogicalResourceId'] for r in response['StackResources'] if r['ResourceStatus'] == 'DELETE_FAILED'] if failed: print(f"Retaining failed resources: {failed}") cf.delete_stack(StackName=stack_name, RetainResources=failed) time.sleep(30) return False # Usage for Django infrastructure cleanup safe_delete_stack('anihpj-django-app')

describe-stacks

Returns detailed information about CloudFormation stacks including status, outputs, parameters, and resource information.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string): Name or ARN of specific stack (omit to list all stacks)
  • --query (string): JMESPath query to filter output
  • --output (string): Output format (json, text, table)
Return Value
  • Stacks (list): Array of stack objects with status, parameters, outputs, tags
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stacks Examples
Example 1: Get Django Stack Details and Outputs

Retrieve stack information including EC2 IP, RDS endpoint, and other outputs for Django configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Get complete stack details
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Stacks[0]' \
  --output json
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/1a2b3c4d", "StackName": "anihpj-django-app", "CreationTime": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z", "StackStatus": "CREATE_COMPLETE", "DisableRollback": false, "Outputs": [ { "OutputKey": "DjangoInstancePublicIP", "OutputValue": "54.123.45.67", "Description": "Public IP of Django EC2 instance" }, { "OutputKey": "DatabaseEndpoint", "OutputValue": "anihpj-db.c9abc12def34.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com", "Description": "RDS database endpoint" }, { "OutputKey": "LoadBalancerURL", "OutputValue": "anihpj-alb-1234567890.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com", "Description": "Application Load Balancer DNS" } ], "Parameters": [ { "ParameterKey": "InstanceType", "ParameterValue": "t3.medium" }, { "ParameterKey": "DBInstanceClass", "ParameterValue": "db.t3.micro" } ], "Tags": [ { "Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj" }, { "Key": "Environment", "Value": "production" } ] }
# Django script to auto-configure from CloudFormation outputs import boto3 import os cf = boto3.client('cloudformation') def get_stack_outputs(stack_name): """Retrieve CloudFormation outputs for Django settings""" response = cf.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) stack = response['Stacks'][0] outputs = {o['OutputKey']: o['OutputValue'] for o in stack.get('Outputs', [])} return outputs # Auto-configure Django from CloudFormation outputs = get_stack_outputs('anihpj-django-app') # Update environment variables os.environ['DJANGO_DB_HOST'] = outputs['DatabaseEndpoint'] os.environ['DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS'] = outputs['LoadBalancerURL'] print(f"✓ Configured Django with CloudFormation outputs") print(f" Database: {outputs['DatabaseEndpoint']}") print(f" Load Balancer: {outputs['LoadBalancerURL']}") # settings.py can now use these outputs ALLOWED_HOSTS = [os.environ.get('DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS')] DATABASES['default']['HOST'] = os.environ.get('DJANGO_DB_HOST')
Example 2: Check Stack Status for CI/CD Pipeline

Monitor stack status in deployment pipeline to determine if infrastructure is ready.

#!/bin/bash
# Check if stack creation/update is complete
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text)

echo "Stack Status: $STATUS"

case $STATUS in
  "CREATE_COMPLETE"|"UPDATE_COMPLETE")
    echo "✓ Stack is ready for deployment"
    exit 0
    ;;
  "CREATE_IN_PROGRESS"|"UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS")
    echo "⏳ Stack operation in progress, waiting..."
    exit 1
    ;;
  "CREATE_FAILED"|"UPDATE_FAILED"|"ROLLBACK_COMPLETE")
    echo "✗ Stack operation failed"
    exit 2
    ;;
esac
Stack Status: CREATE_COMPLETE ✓ Stack is ready for deployment Proceeding with Django application deployment... 1. SSH to EC2: 54.123.45.67 2. Database available: anihpj-db.c9abc12def34.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com 3. Running migrations... 4. Collecting static files... 5. Restarting Gunicorn... ✓ Django application deployed successfully
Example 3: Extract All Stack Outputs to Environment File

Generate .env file for Django from CloudFormation stack outputs.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate Django .env file from CloudFormation outputs
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

# Get all outputs
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs[*].[OutputKey,OutputValue]' \
  --output text | while read -r KEY VALUE; do
    echo "$KEY=$VALUE"
done > .env

echo "✓ Generated .env file from CloudFormation outputs"
cat .env
✓ Generated .env file from CloudFormation outputs DjangoInstancePublicIP=54.123.45.67 DatabaseEndpoint=anihpj-db.c9abc12def34.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com DatabasePort=5432 LoadBalancerURL=anihpj-alb-1234567890.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com S3MediaBucket=anihpj-jobpost-media-prod CloudFrontDomain=d1234abcd.cloudfront.net RedisEndpoint=anihpj-redis.abc123.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com
# Django settings.py using .env from CloudFormation from dotenv import load_dotenv import os # Load CloudFormation outputs from .env load_dotenv() # Database configuration from CloudFormation DATABASES = { 'default': { 'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql', 'NAME': 'anihpj_jobpost', 'USER': 'anihpj_admin', 'PASSWORD': os.getenv('DB_PASSWORD'), 'HOST': os.getenv('DatabaseEndpoint'), # From CloudFormation 'PORT': os.getenv('DatabasePort', '5432'), } } # S3 configuration from CloudFormation AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = os.getenv('S3MediaBucket') AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = os.getenv('CloudFrontDomain') # Cache configuration from CloudFormation CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache', 'LOCATION': f"redis://{os.getenv('RedisEndpoint')}:6379/1", } } # Allowed hosts from CloudFormation ALLOWED_HOSTS = [ os.getenv('DjangoInstancePublicIP'), os.getenv('LoadBalancerURL'), 'anihpj.com' ]
Example 4: Get Stack Parameters for Audit

Review all parameters used to create the stack for compliance and documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract stack parameters for audit log
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Stacks[0].Parameters[*].[ParameterKey,ParameterValue]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------- | DescribeStacks | +--------------------+-----------------+ | InstanceType | t3.medium | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.micro | | KeyPairName | anihpj-key | | VpcCIDR | 10.0.0.0/16 | | Environment | production | | MultiAZ | true | | BackupRetention | 7 | +--------------------+-----------------+ Audit Report Generated: 2026-01-17 Stack: anihpj-django-app Compliant with security policy: ✓ - Multi-AZ enabled - Backup retention ≥7 days - Instance types within approved list
Example 5: Monitor Multiple Stacks Status Dashboard

Create monitoring dashboard showing status of all related CloudFormation stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor all anihpj stacks
echo "=== anihpj Infrastructure Status ==="
echo ""

for STACK in anihpj-vpc anihpj-django-app anihpj-storage-cdn anihpj-monitoring; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ -n "$STATUS" ]; then
    echo "[$STACK] $STATUS"
  else
    echo "[$STACK] NOT_FOUND"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Resource Summary ==="
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --query 'Stacks[*].[StackName,length(Outputs)]' \
  --output table
=== anihpj Infrastructure Status === [anihpj-vpc] CREATE_COMPLETE [anihpj-django-app] UPDATE_COMPLETE [anihpj-storage-cdn] CREATE_COMPLETE [anihpj-monitoring] CREATE_COMPLETE === Resource Summary === ----------------------------------------- | DescribeStacks | +---------------------------+-----------+ | anihpj-vpc | 4 | | anihpj-django-app | 7 | | anihpj-storage-cdn | 3 | | anihpj-monitoring | 2 | +---------------------------+-----------+ All infrastructure stacks operational ✓ Total outputs: 16 Health status: GREEN
# Python dashboard for Django admin showing CloudFormation status from django.contrib import admin from django.utils.html import format_html import boto3 class InfrastructureStatusAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): """Admin view showing CloudFormation stack status""" def changelist_view(self, request, extra_context=None): cf = boto3.client('cloudformation') stacks = ['anihpj-vpc', 'anihpj-django-app', 'anihpj-storage-cdn', 'anihpj-monitoring'] stack_status = [] for stack_name in stacks: try: response = cf.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) stack = response['Stacks'][0] stack_status.append({ 'name': stack_name, 'status': stack['StackStatus'], 'created': stack['CreationTime'], 'outputs': len(stack.get('Outputs', [])) }) except: stack_status.append({ 'name': stack_name, 'status': 'NOT_FOUND', 'created': None, 'outputs': 0 }) extra_context = extra_context or {} extra_context['stack_status'] = stack_status return super().changelist_view(request, extra_context)

list-stacks

Returns summary information about all CloudFormation stacks in your account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stacks --stack-status-filter {status}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-status-filter (list): Filter by status (CREATE_COMPLETE, DELETE_COMPLETE, etc.)
  • --query (string): JMESPath query to filter results
Return Value
  • StackSummaries (list): Array of stack summaries with name, status, creation time
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stacks Examples
Example 1: List All Active Stacks

View all currently active CloudFormation stacks for the anihpj project.

#!/bin/bash
# List all active stacks
aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[*].[StackName,StackStatus,CreationTime]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStacks | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ | anihpj-vpc | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-10T14:30:00Z | | anihpj-django-app | UPDATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-12T09:15:00Z | | anihpj-storage-cdn | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-13T16:45:00Z | | anihpj-monitoring | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-15T11:20:00Z | | anihpj-django-dev | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-16T08:00:00Z | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ Total active stacks: 5
Example 2: Find Failed or In-Progress Stacks

Identify stacks that need attention due to failures or ongoing operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Find problematic stacks
aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter \
    CREATE_FAILED \
    UPDATE_FAILED \
    ROLLBACK_COMPLETE \
    CREATE_IN_PROGRESS \
    UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS \
  --query 'StackSummaries[*].[StackName,StackStatus,StackStatusReason]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStacks | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ | anihpj-test-stack | CREATE_FAILED | Insufficient IAM | | | | permissions | | anihpj-django-staging | UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS | - | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ ⚠ 2 stacks require attention: 1. anihpj-test-stack: Failed due to IAM permissions Action: Update IAM role and retry 2. anihpj-django-staging: Update in progress Action: Monitor completion (ETA: 5 minutes)
Example 3: Count Stacks by Status

Generate statistics showing distribution of stack statuses.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate stack status statistics
echo "=== CloudFormation Stack Statistics ==="
echo ""

for STATUS in CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE DELETE_COMPLETE \
              CREATE_FAILED UPDATE_FAILED ROLLBACK_COMPLETE; do
  COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
    --stack-status-filter $STATUS \
    --query 'length(StackSummaries)' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "$STATUS: $COUNT"
done
=== CloudFormation Stack Statistics === CREATE_COMPLETE: 12 UPDATE_COMPLETE: 5 DELETE_COMPLETE: 23 CREATE_FAILED: 2 UPDATE_FAILED: 1 ROLLBACK_COMPLETE: 1 Total stacks (active): 18 Total stacks (deleted): 23 Success rate: 89% (18 successful out of 20 attempts)
Example 4: List Stacks Created in Last 30 Days

Find recently created stacks for cost analysis and review.

#!/bin/bash
# List stacks created in last 30 days
CUTOFF_DATE=$(date -u -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query "StackSummaries[?CreationTime>=\`$CUTOFF_DATE\`].[StackName,CreationTime]" \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStacks | +---------------------------+-------------------------------------+ | anihpj-monitoring | 2026-01-15T11:20:00Z (2 days ago) | | anihpj-django-dev | 2026-01-16T08:00:00Z (1 day ago) | +---------------------------+-------------------------------------+ Recent stack deployments: 2 Cost impact: ~$85/month (estimated) Review for: Optimization opportunities
Example 5: Export Stack Inventory to CSV

Generate comprehensive inventory report of all CloudFormation stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Export stack inventory to CSV
echo "StackName,Status,CreationTime,LastUpdated" > cloudformation-inventory.csv

aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[*].[StackName,StackStatus,CreationTime,LastUpdatedTime]' \
  --output text | while read -r NAME STATUS CREATED UPDATED; do
    echo "$NAME,$STATUS,$CREATED,$UPDATED"
done >> cloudformation-inventory.csv

echo "✓ Inventory exported to cloudformation-inventory.csv"
✓ Inventory exported to cloudformation-inventory.csv Sample contents: StackName,Status,CreationTime,LastUpdated anihpj-vpc,CREATE_COMPLETE,2026-01-10T14:30:00Z,2026-01-10T14:45:00Z anihpj-django-app,UPDATE_COMPLETE,2026-01-12T09:15:00Z,2026-01-17T10:30:00Z anihpj-storage-cdn,CREATE_COMPLETE,2026-01-13T16:45:00Z,2026-01-13T17:00:00Z Total records: 5 File size: 1.2 KB
# Django management command to import CloudFormation inventory from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand import boto3 import csv class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Import CloudFormation stack inventory' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf = boto3.client('cloudformation') # Get all active stacks response = cf.list_stacks( StackStatusFilter=['CREATE_COMPLETE', 'UPDATE_COMPLETE'] ) # Save to database (assuming you have a CloudFormationStack model) for stack_summary in response['StackSummaries']: CloudFormationStack.objects.update_or_create( stack_name=stack_summary['StackName'], defaults={ 'status': stack_summary['StackStatus'], 'creation_time': stack_summary['CreationTime'], 'last_updated': stack_summary.get('LastUpdatedTime'), } ) self.stdout.write( self.style.SUCCESS( f'✓ Imported {len(response["StackSummaries"])} stacks' ) )

validate-template

Validates CloudFormation template syntax and returns parameter information. Use before stack creation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation validate-template --template-body file://template.yaml
Parameters Description
  • --template-body (string): Path to local template file
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of template
Return Value
  • Parameters (list): Template parameters with defaults and descriptions
  • Capabilities (list): Required capabilities (IAM, NAMED_IAM)
5 Detailed CloudFormation validate-template Examples
Example 1: Validate Django Infrastructure Template

Check template syntax before creating production infrastructure.

#!/bin/bash
# Validate template before deployment
aws cloudformation validate-template \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --output json
{ "Parameters": [ { "ParameterKey": "InstanceType", "DefaultValue": "t3.medium", "NoEcho": false, "Description": "EC2 instance type for Django application" }, { "ParameterKey": "DBInstanceClass", "DefaultValue": "db.t3.micro", "NoEcho": false, "Description": "RDS instance class" }, { "ParameterKey": "KeyPairName", "NoEcho": false, "Description": "EC2 key pair name" } ], "Capabilities": [ "CAPABILITY_IAM" ], "CapabilitiesReason": "Template contains IAM resources" } ✓ Template validation successful ✓ No syntax errors found ✓ Required capability: CAPABILITY_IAM
Example 2: Validate and Extract Parameter Defaults

Review all template parameters and their default values for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract parameter information from template
aws cloudformation validate-template \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --query 'Parameters[*].[ParameterKey,DefaultValue,Description]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ValidateTemplate | +--------------------+------------------+-------------------------------------+ | InstanceType | t3.medium | EC2 instance type for Django | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.micro | RDS instance class | | KeyPairName | | EC2 key pair name (required) | | VpcCIDR | 10.0.0.0/16 | VPC CIDR block | | Environment | production | Environment name | +--------------------+------------------+-------------------------------------+ Parameter Summary: - Total parameters: 5 - Required parameters: 1 (KeyPairName) - Optional parameters: 4 (have defaults)
Example 3: Validate Template with Error Handling

Check for template errors in CI/CD pipeline before deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Validate template with error handling for CI/CD
TEMPLATE_FILE="django-infrastructure.yaml"

if aws cloudformation validate-template \
    --template-body file://$TEMPLATE_FILE \
    --output json > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    
    echo "✓ Template validation passed: $TEMPLATE_FILE"
    echo "✓ Safe to proceed with stack creation"
    exit 0
else
    echo "✗ Template validation failed: $TEMPLATE_FILE"
    echo "✗ Fix errors before deployment"
    
    # Show detailed error
    aws cloudformation validate-template \
        --template-body file://$TEMPLATE_FILE 2>&1
    
    exit 1
fi
✗ Template validation failed: django-infrastructure.yaml ✗ Fix errors before deployment An error occurred (ValidationError) when calling the ValidateTemplate operation: Template format error: Unresolved resource dependencies [VPCId] in the Resources block of the template Error details: - Line 45: Resource 'DjangoSecurityGroup' references undefined VPCId - Suggested fix: Define VPC resource or add VPCId as parameter CI/CD Pipeline: FAILED Action required: Fix template and retry
Example 4: Pre-deployment Validation Script

Comprehensive validation including template syntax, parameter files, and IAM capabilities.

#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive pre-deployment validation
TEMPLATE="django-infrastructure.yaml"
PARAMS="parameters.json"

echo "=== CloudFormation Pre-Deployment Validation ==="
echo ""

# 1. Validate template syntax
echo "[1/3] Validating template syntax..."
VALIDATION=$(aws cloudformation validate-template \
  --template-body file://$TEMPLATE \
  --output json)

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Template syntax valid"
else
  echo "✗ Template syntax invalid"
  exit 1
fi

# 2. Check IAM capabilities requirement
echo "[2/3] Checking IAM capabilities..."
CAPABILITIES=$(echo $VALIDATION | jq -r '.Capabilities[]' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$CAPABILITIES" ]; then
  echo "✓ Required capabilities: $CAPABILITIES"
else
  echo "✓ No special capabilities required"
fi

# 3. Validate parameter file
echo "[3/3] Validating parameter file..."
if jq empty $PARAMS 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "✓ Parameter file is valid JSON"
else
  echo "✗ Parameter file is invalid"
  exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "✓ All validations passed - ready for deployment"
=== CloudFormation Pre-Deployment Validation === [1/3] Validating template syntax... ✓ Template syntax valid [2/3] Checking IAM capabilities... ✓ Required capabilities: CAPABILITY_IAM [3/3] Validating parameter file... ✓ Parameter file is valid JSON ✓ All validations passed - ready for deployment Deployment checklist: ✓ Template validated ✓ IAM capability noted ✓ Parameters verified ✓ Ready to execute: create-stack
Example 5: Validate Multiple Templates in Directory

Batch validate all CloudFormation templates for anihpj project.

#!/bin/bash
# Validate all YAML/JSON templates in directory
TEMPLATE_DIR="./cloudformation-templates"

echo "=== Validating CloudFormation Templates ==="
echo ""

PASSED=0
FAILED=0

for TEMPLATE in $TEMPLATE_DIR/*.{yaml,yml,json}; do
  if [ -f "$TEMPLATE" ]; then
    FILENAME=$(basename "$TEMPLATE")
    
    if aws cloudformation validate-template \
        --template-body file://$TEMPLATE \
        --output json > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        
      echo "✓ $FILENAME"
      ((PASSED++))
    else
      echo "✗ $FILENAME"
      ((FAILED++))
    fi
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"

[ $FAILED -eq 0 ] && exit 0 || exit 1
=== Validating CloudFormation Templates === ✓ vpc-infrastructure.yaml ✓ django-application.yaml ✓ rds-database.yaml ✓ s3-storage.yaml ✗ monitoring-stack.yaml (syntax error) ✓ networking.yaml ✓ security-groups.yaml Results: 6 passed, 1 failed Failed template details: monitoring-stack.yaml: Error: Invalid intrinsic function Fn::GetAtt reference Line 34: !GetAtt NonExistentResource.Arn Action required: Fix monitoring-stack.yaml before deployment
# Django management command to validate templates from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand import boto3 import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Validate all CloudFormation templates' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument( '--directory', type=str, default='./cloudformation', help='Directory containing templates' ) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf = boto3.client('cloudformation') template_dir = options['directory'] results = {'passed': [], 'failed': []} for filename in os.listdir(template_dir): if filename.endswith(('.yaml', '.yml', '.json')): filepath = os.path.join(template_dir, filename) try: with open(filepath, 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() response = cf.validate_template(TemplateBody=template_body) results['passed'].append(filename) self.stdout.write( self.style.SUCCESS(f'✓ {filename}') ) except Exception as e: results['failed'].append((filename, str(e))) self.stdout.write( self.style.ERROR(f'✗ {filename}: {str(e)}') ) self.stdout.write('') self.stdout.write( f"Results: {len(results['passed'])} passed, " f"{len(results['failed'])} failed" )

create-change-set

Creates a change set to preview changes before updating a stack. Review changes before executing.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-change-set \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --change-set-name {change-set-name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name of the stack to update
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Unique name for the change set
  • --template-body (string): Path to new template file
  • --parameters (list): New parameter values
  • --change-set-type (string): CREATE (new stack) or UPDATE (existing stack)
Return Value
  • Id (string): Change set ARN
  • StackId (string): Stack ARN
3 CloudFormation create-change-set Examples
Example 1: Create Change Set for Instance Type Update
#!/bin/bash
# Preview instance type change before applying
aws cloudformation create-change-set \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,UsePreviousValue=true

echo "Change set created - review before executing"
{ "Id": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:changeSet/upgrade-to-t3-large/1a2b3c4d", "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/abcd1234" } Change set created - review before executing Status: CREATE_COMPLETE To review changes: aws cloudformation describe-change-set --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large --stack-name anihpj-django-app To execute: aws cloudformation execute-change-set --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large --stack-name anihpj-django-app
Example 2: Create Change Set for New Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Preview adding CloudWatch alarms to stack
aws cloudformation create-change-set \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --template-body file://django-with-monitoring.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,UsePreviousValue=true \
    ParameterKey=AlarmEmail,ParameterValue=admin@anihpj.com \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

# Wait for change set creation
aws cloudformation wait change-set-create-complete \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app
{ "Id": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:changeSet/add-monitoring/5e6f7g8h", "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/abcd1234" } Change set creation complete Changes preview: + SNS Topic (AlarmTopic) + CloudWatch Alarm (HighCPUAlarm) + CloudWatch Alarm (LowDiskSpaceAlarm) + Lambda Function (AlertProcessor) Total: 4 resources to add, 0 to modify, 0 to remove
Example 3: Create Change Set with Tag
#!/bin/bash
# Create change set with description and tags
aws cloudformation create-change-set \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name scale-up-production \
  --use-previous-template \
  --description "Scale up for increased traffic" \
  --parameters ParameterKey=DesiredCapacity,ParameterValue=5 \
  --tags Key=ChangeType,Value=Scaling Key=Approver,Value=DevOps
{ "Id": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:changeSet/scale-up-production/9i0j1k2l", "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/abcd1234" } Change set details: Name: scale-up-production Description: Scale up for increased traffic Status: CREATE_COMPLETE Tags: ChangeType=Scaling, Approver=DevOps Estimated cost impact: +$45/month

describe-change-set

Returns detailed information about a change set including all proposed changes.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name {name} \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Name or ARN of change set
  • --stack-name (string): Stack name (required if change-set-name is not ARN)
Return Value
  • Changes (list): Detailed list of all proposed changes
  • Status (string): Change set status
3 CloudFormation describe-change-set Examples
Example 1: Review Change Set Before Execution
#!/bin/bash
# Review all changes before applying
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Changes[*].[Type,ResourceChange.Action,ResourceChange.LogicalResourceId,ResourceChange.Replacement]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSet | +----------------+----------+----------------------+---------------------------+ | ResourceChange | Modify | DjangoInstance | True (requires replacement)| | ResourceChange | Add | AlarmTopic | False | +----------------+----------+----------------------+---------------------------+ Change details: 1. DjangoInstance will be REPLACED (new instance created, old terminated) - InstanceType: t3.medium → t3.large - Impact: Brief downtime during replacement 2. AlarmTopic will be ADDED - New SNS topic for CloudWatch alarms Review complete - Safe to execute
Example 2: Check Change Set Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check if change set is ready to execute
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

echo "Change set status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]; then
  echo "✓ Ready to execute"
else
  echo "⏳ Not ready - wait for completion"
fi
Change set status: CREATE_COMPLETE ✓ Ready to execute Change set summary: - Created: 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z - Changes: 4 additions, 0 modifications, 0 deletions - Execution time: ~5 minutes - Rollback available: Yes
Example 3: Extract Change Details for Approval
#!/bin/bash
# Generate approval document with change details
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name scale-up-production \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json > change-set-details.json

# Extract key information
echo "=== Change Set Approval Request ==="
jq -r '.Changes[] | "[\(.ResourceChange.Action)] \(.ResourceChange.LogicalResourceId) - \(.ResourceChange.ResourceType)"' \
  change-set-details.json
=== Change Set Approval Request === [Modify] AutoScalingGroup - AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup Property changes: - DesiredCapacity: 3 → 5 - Impact: 2 new EC2 instances launched [Modify] TargetGroup - AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup Property changes: - HealthCheckInterval: 30s → 15s (improved monitoring) Estimated monthly cost increase: +$45 Approval recommended: Yes Risk level: Low

execute-change-set

Executes a change set to apply the proposed changes to the stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name {name} \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Name or ARN of change set to execute
  • --stack-name (string): Stack name (required if change-set-name is not ARN)
  • --disable-rollback: Disable rollback on update failure
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
3 CloudFormation execute-change-set Examples
Example 1: Execute Change Set and Monitor
#!/bin/bash
# Execute change set and wait for completion
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "Executing change set..."

# Wait for update to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "✓ Stack update completed successfully"
Executing change set... UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS Progress: [=====> ] 33% - Creating new instance (t3.large) [==========> ] 66% - Updating load balancer targets [===============>] 100% - Terminating old instance ✓ Stack update completed successfully Duration: 8 minutes Resources updated: 3 Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE
Example 2: Execute with Event Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Execute and monitor events in real-time
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

# Monitor stack events
watch -n 5 'aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 5 \
  --query "StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]" \
  --output table'
Every 5.0s: aws cloudformation describe-stack-events... ------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackEvents | +----------------------+------------------+-----------------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AlarmTopic | | 2026-01-17T10:45:15Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | HighCPUAlarm | | 2026-01-17T10:45:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | SNSSubscription | +----------------------+------------------+-----------------------+ Change set execution in progress... Resources created: 3/4
Example 3: Execute Multiple Change Sets in Sequence
#!/bin/bash
# Execute multiple change sets for phased deployment
CHANGE_SETS=("phase1-networking" "phase2-compute" "phase3-monitoring")

for CS in "${CHANGE_SETS[@]}"; do
  echo "Executing: $CS"
  
  aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
    --change-set-name $CS \
    --stack-name anihpj-django-app
  
  # Wait for completion before next phase
  aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-django-app
  
  echo "✓ $CS completed"
done

echo "✓ All phases deployed successfully"
Executing: phase1-networking ✓ phase1-networking completed (Duration: 4 minutes) Executing: phase2-compute ✓ phase2-compute completed (Duration: 6 minutes) Executing: phase3-monitoring ✓ phase3-monitoring completed (Duration: 2 minutes) ✓ All phases deployed successfully Total deployment time: 12 minutes Infrastructure ready for Django deployment

delete-change-set

Deletes a change set without executing it. Use when changes are no longer needed.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
  --change-set-name {name} \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Name or ARN of change set to delete
  • --stack-name (string): Stack name
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
2 CloudFormation delete-change-set Examples
Example 1: Delete Rejected Change Set
#!/bin/bash
# Delete change set after review rejection
aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
  --change-set-name risky-update \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "Change set deleted - no changes applied"
Change set deleted - no changes applied Stack remains unchanged Status: No action taken
Example 2: Clean Up Old Change Sets
#!/bin/bash
# Delete all change sets for a stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

CHANGE_SETS=$(aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[*].ChangeSetName' \
  --output text)

for CS in $CHANGE_SETS; do
  aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
    --change-set-name $CS \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME
  echo "Deleted: $CS"
done
Deleted: old-update-2026-01-10 Deleted: test-changes-2026-01-12 Deleted: deprecated-config-2026-01-15 ✓ Cleaned up 3 old change sets Stack is clean - only active configuration remains

list-change-sets

Lists all change sets for a specific stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-change-sets --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Stack name to list change sets for
Return Value
  • Summaries (list): Array of change set summaries
2 CloudFormation list-change-sets Examples
Example 1: List All Change Sets
#!/bin/bash
# List all change sets for stack
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[ChangeSetName,Status,CreationTime]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListChangeSets | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ | upgrade-to-t3-large | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | | add-monitoring | EXECUTED | 2026-01-16T14:30:00Z | | scale-up-production | CREATE_COMPLETE | 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z | +---------------------------+------------------+--------------------+ Total change sets: 3 Pending execution: 2 Already executed: 1
Example 2: Find Pending Change Sets
#!/bin/bash
# List only pending (not executed) change sets
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`CREATE_COMPLETE`].[ChangeSetName,Description]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListChangeSets | +---------------------------+---------------------------------------+ | upgrade-to-t3-large | Increase instance size for performance| | scale-up-production | Scale up for increased traffic | +---------------------------+---------------------------------------+ Pending approvals: 2 Action required: Review and execute or delete

📦 Stack Sets Operations

create-stack-set

Creates a stack set to deploy stacks across multiple AWS accounts and regions.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Unique name for the stack set
  • --template-body (string): Path to template file
  • --parameters (list): Parameters for all stack instances
  • --capabilities (list): IAM capabilities if needed
Return Value
  • StackSetId (string): Unique identifier for the stack set
2 CloudFormation create-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Create Multi-Region Django Stack Set
#!/bin/bash
# Create stack set for multi-region deployment
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-regional.yaml \
  --description "Django application across multiple regions" \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-multi-region-django:1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890" } ✓ Stack set created Ready to deploy instances to multiple regions Next: create-stack-instances
Example 2: Create Multi-Account Stack Set
#!/bin/bash
# Create stack set for multi-account deployment
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-org-wide-security \
  --template-body file://security-baseline.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-org-wide-security:9i8j7k6l-5m4n-3o2p-1q0r-abcdefghijkl" } ✓ Stack set created with AWS Organizations integration Auto-deployment enabled for new accounts

update-stack-set

Updates a stack set with new template or parameters across all instances.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of stack set to update
  • --template-body (string): New template file
  • --parameters (list): Updated parameters
  • --operation-preferences: Control how updates are applied
Return Value
  • OperationId (string): Unique ID for the update operation
2 CloudFormation update-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Update Stack Set with Rolling Update
#!/bin/bash
# Update stack set across all regions
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-regional-v2.yaml \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=SEQUENTIAL \
    MaxConcurrentPercentage=25 \
    FailureTolerancePercentage=10
{ "OperationId": "abc12345-de67-89fg-hijk-lmnopqrstuvw" } Update operation started Strategy: Rolling update (25% at a time) Failure tolerance: 10% Regions: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1
Example 2: Update Parameters Only
#!/bin/bash
# Update parameters without changing template
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large
{ "OperationId": "xyz98765-vu43-21ts-rqpo-nmlkjihgfedcb" } Parameter update in progress Updating all stack instances with new InstanceType Instances affected: 9 (across 3 regions)

delete-stack-set

Deletes a stack set. All stack instances must be deleted first.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name {name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of stack set to delete
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
1 CloudFormation delete-stack-set Example
Example: Delete Stack Set After Removing Instances
#!/bin/bash
# First delete all instances, then delete stack set
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 \
  --no-retain-stacks

# Wait for instances to be deleted
sleep 60

# Delete the stack set
aws cloudformation delete-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django

echo "✓ Stack set deleted"
Deleting stack instances... ✓ us-east-1 instance deleted ✓ us-west-2 instance deleted ✓ Stack set deleted All resources removed

describe-stack-set

Returns information about a stack set including parameters and status.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set --stack-set-name {name}
2 CloudFormation describe-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Get Stack Set Details
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --output json
{ "StackSet": { "StackSetName": "anihpj-multi-region-django", "Status": "ACTIVE", "StackSetId": "anihpj-multi-region-django:1a2b3c4d", "Description": "Django application across multiple regions" } }
Example 2: Check Stack Set Status
#!/bin/bash
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --query 'StackSet.Status' \
  --output text)

echo "Stack Set Status: $STATUS"
Stack Set Status: ACTIVE ✓ Ready for operations

list-stack-sets

Lists all stack sets in your account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets
1 CloudFormation list-stack-sets Example
Example: List All Stack Sets
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[StackSetName,Status]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------- | ListStackSets | +-------------------------------+----------+ | anihpj-multi-region-django | ACTIVE | | anihpj-org-wide-security | ACTIVE | +-------------------------------+----------+

create-stack-instances

Creates stack instances in specified accounts and regions from a stack set.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --accounts {account-id} \
  --regions {region}
2 CloudFormation create-stack-instances Examples
Example 1: Deploy to Multiple Regions
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1 \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount=2
{ "OperationId": "op-123abc" } Creating instances in 3 regions us-east-1: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS us-west-2: PENDING eu-west-1: PENDING
Example 2: Deploy with Parameter Overrides
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions ap-southeast-1 \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.small
{ "OperationId": "op-456def" } Creating instance in ap-southeast-1 with custom parameters InstanceType: t3.small (overridden from t3.medium)

update-stack-instances

Updates stack instances with parameter overrides.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --accounts {account-id} \
  --regions {region} \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value}
1 CloudFormation update-stack-instances Example
Example: Update Regional Parameters
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-west-2 \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large
{ "OperationId": "op-789ghi" } Updating us-west-2 instance Parameter change: t3.medium → t3.large

delete-stack-instances

Deletes stack instances from specified accounts and regions.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --accounts {account-id} \
  --regions {region} \
  --no-retain-stacks
1 CloudFormation delete-stack-instances Example
Example: Remove Regional Instances
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions eu-west-1 \
  --no-retain-stacks
{ "OperationId": "op-012jkl" } Deleting instance in eu-west-1 All resources will be removed

list-stack-instances

Lists all stack instances belonging to a stack set.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances --stack-set-name {name}
1 CloudFormation list-stack-instances Example
Example: List All Instances
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Region,Account,Status]' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------- | ListStackInstances | +---------------+---------------+------------------+ | us-east-1 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | | us-west-2 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | | eu-west-1 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | +---------------+---------------+------------------+

🔍 Drift Detection

detect-stack-drift

Initiates drift detection to identify differences between expected and actual resource configuration.

Syntax
aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift --stack-name {stack-name}
2 CloudFormation detect-stack-drift Examples
Example 1: Detect Configuration Drift
#!/bin/bash
# Start drift detection
DRIFT_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackDriftDetectionId' \
  --output text)

echo "Drift detection initiated: $DRIFT_ID"

# Wait for detection to complete
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id $DRIFT_ID
Drift detection initiated: abc123-def456-ghi789 { "StackDriftStatus": "DRIFTED", "DriftedStackResourceCount": 2, "DetectionStatus": "DETECTION_COMPLETE" } ⚠ Stack has drifted from template 2 resources modified outside CloudFormation
Example 2: Automated Drift Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Regular drift check for compliance
for STACK in anihpj-vpc anihpj-django-app anihpj-storage; do
  echo "Checking drift: $STACK"
  aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift --stack-name $STACK
done
Checking drift: anihpj-vpc ✓ No drift detected Checking drift: anihpj-django-app ⚠ Drift detected (2 resources) Checking drift: anihpj-storage ✓ No drift detected Compliance check complete

describe-stack-drift-detection-status

Returns status information about a drift detection operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id {detection-id}
1 CloudFormation describe-stack-drift-detection-status Example
Example: Check Detection Status
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id abc123-def456 \
  --query '[DetectionStatus,StackDriftStatus,DriftedStackResourceCount]' \
  --output text
DETECTION_COMPLETE DRIFTED 2 Drift Summary: - Status: Detection complete - Result: Stack has drifted - Drifted resources: 2

describe-stack-resource-drifts

Returns detailed drift information for each resource in a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts --stack-name {stack-name}
2 CloudFormation describe-stack-resource-drifts Examples
Example 1: View Drifted Resources
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED \
  --query 'StackResourceDrifts[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,StackResourceDriftStatus]' \
  --output table
