---------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackResourceDrifts | +---------------------+-------------------------+--------------------+ | DjangoSecurityGroup | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | MODIFIED | | DjangoInstance | AWS::EC2::Instance | MODIFIED | +---------------------+-------------------------+--------------------+ Drifted resources: 1. DjangoSecurityGroup: Ingress rules modified manually 2. DjangoInstance: Tags changed outside CloudFormation
Example 2: Export Drift Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate drift report
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json > drift-report.json

echo "Drift report saved to drift-report.json"
Drift report saved to drift-report.json Report contains: - Resource-level drift details - Expected vs actual configurations - Property differences - Timestamps of last drift detection

⚙️ Stack Operations

continue-update-rollback

Continues rolling back an update after it pauses in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state.

Syntax
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback --stack-name {stack-name}
1 CloudFormation continue-update-rollback Example
Example: Resume Failed Rollback
#!/bin/bash
# Resume rollback after failure
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --resources-to-skip DjangoInstance

echo "Rollback resumed, skipping problematic resources"
Rollback resumed, skipping problematic resources Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Skipped: DjangoInstance (will be manually fixed)

cancel-update-stack

Cancels an update on a stack in UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS state.

Syntax
aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name {stack-name}
1 CloudFormation cancel-update-stack Example
Example: Cancel In-Progress Update
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name anihpj-django-app
echo "Update canceled, rolling back changes"
Update canceled, rolling back changes Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Rolling back to previous stable state

set-stack-policy

Sets a stack policy that protects resources from being updated or deleted.

Syntax
aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --stack-policy-body file://policy.json
1 CloudFormation set-stack-policy Example
Example: Protect Database from Deletion
#!/bin/bash
# Protect RDS database from accidental deletion
cat > stack-policy.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": "Update:Delete",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-body file://stack-policy.json

echo "✓ Database protected from deletion"
✓ Database protected from deletion Policy applied to: anihpj-django-app Protected resources: JobPostDatabase Action: Update:Delete now denied

get-stack-policy

Returns the stack policy for a specified stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy --stack-name {stack-name}
1 CloudFormation get-stack-policy Example
Example: View Stack Policy
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' \
  --output text | jq
{ "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Deny", "Action": "Update:Delete", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase" } ] } Policy Summary: - JobPostDatabase: Protected from deletion - All other resources: Unrestricted

estimate-template-cost

Returns URL to AWS Simple Monthly Calculator with pre-populated template resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost --template-body file://template.yaml
1 CloudFormation estimate-template-cost Example
Example: Estimate Infrastructure Cost
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
  --query 'Url' \
  --output text
https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=abc123def456 Cost Estimation URL generated Opens AWS Pricing Calculator with: - EC2 t3.medium instance - RDS db.t3.micro - VPC and networking - Estimated monthly cost: ~$85-$120

describe-stack-events

Returns all stack-related events for a specified stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events --stack-name {stack-name}
2 CloudFormation describe-stack-events Examples
Example 1: View Recent Events
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 10 \
  --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackEvents | +-----------------------+------------------+------------------+------------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::Instance | DjangoInstance | | 2026-01-17T10:45:15Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | AWS::EC2::Instance | DjangoInstance | | 2026-01-17T10:45:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | DjangoSG | +-----------------------+------------------+------------------+------------------+
Example 2: Monitor Stack Creation
#!/bin/bash
# Real-time event monitoring
watch -n 5 'aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 5 \
  --query "StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]" \
  --output table'
Every 5.0s: aws cloudformation describe-stack-events... --------------------------------------------------------------- | 2026-01-17T10:47:00Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | VPC | | 2026-01-17T10:46:45Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | InternetGateway | | 2026-01-17T10:46:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | Subnet | --------------------------------------------------------------- Stack creation: 45% complete

describe-stack-resources

Returns AWS resource descriptions for running resources in a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources --stack-name {stack-name}
2 CloudFormation describe-stack-resources Examples
Example 1: List All Stack Resources
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackResources[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,PhysicalResourceId,ResourceStatus]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribeStackResources | +-------------------+-------------------------+----------------------+------------------+ | DjangoInstance | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0abc123def456 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | DjangoSG | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-0123456789abcdef | CREATE_COMPLETE | | JobPostDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db | CREATE_COMPLETE | +-------------------+-------------------------+----------------------+------------------+
Example 2: Get Specific Resource Physical ID
#!/bin/bash
# Get EC2 instance ID
INSTANCE_ID=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --logical-resource-id DjangoInstance \
  --query 'StackResources[0].PhysicalResourceId' \
  --output text)

echo "Django Instance ID: $INSTANCE_ID"
Django Instance ID: i-0abc123def456789 Use for: - SSH access - CloudWatch monitoring - Direct AWS operations

get-template

Returns the template body for a specified stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-template --stack-name {stack-name}
2 CloudFormation get-template Examples
Example 1: Download Current Template
#!/bin/bash
# Download template for backup/review
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output text > current-template.yaml

echo "✓ Template downloaded"
✓ Template downloaded File: current-template.yaml Size: 4,523 bytes Use for: Backup, version control, documentation
Example 2: Compare Template Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Download and compare templates
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output text > deployed-template.yaml

diff deployed-template.yaml local-template.yaml
Comparing templates... Differences found: < InstanceType: t3.medium (deployed) > InstanceType: t3.large (local) Template drift detected

get-template-summary

Returns summary information about a template without creating a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-template-summary --template-body file://template.yaml
1 CloudFormation get-template-summary Example
Example: Preview Template Contents
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --query '[Parameters[*].ParameterKey, ResourceTypes]' \
  --output json
[ ["InstanceType", "DBInstanceClass", "KeyPairName"], ["AWS::EC2::Instance", "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "AWS::EC2::VPC"] ] Template Summary: - Parameters: 3 - Resources: 15 - Resource types: 8 unique types

list-exports

Lists all exported output values from stacks.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-exports
1 CloudFormation list-exports Example
Example: List All Cross-Stack Exports
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query 'Exports[*].[Name,Value,ExportingStackId]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListExports | +-------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+ | anihpj-vpc-id | vpc-0abc123 | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-vpc| | anihpj-db-endpoint | anihpj-db.rds... | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-db | | anihpj-s3-bucket | anihpj-media-prod | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-s3 | +-------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+ Exports available for cross-stack references

list-imports

Lists stacks that are importing an exported output value.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-imports --export-name {export-name}
1 CloudFormation list-imports Example
Example: Find Stacks Using Export
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-imports \
  --export-name anihpj-vpc-id \
  --query 'Imports' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------- | ListImports | +-------------------------------------+ | anihpj-django-app | | anihpj-storage-cdn | | anihpj-monitoring | +-------------------------------------+ 3 stacks depend on: anihpj-vpc-id ⚠ Cannot delete VPC stack while these stacks exist

deploy

High-level command that packages and deploys CloudFormation templates (create or update).

Syntax
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file template.yaml \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
2 CloudFormation deploy Examples
Example 1: Deploy or Update Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Automatically creates or updates stack
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.medium \
    DBInstanceClass=db.t3.micro \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --tags Project=anihpj Environment=production
Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack create/update to complete... Successfully created/updated stack: anihpj-django-app Duration: 12 minutes Status: CREATE_COMPLETE or UPDATE_COMPLETE
Example 2: Deploy with No Execute
#!/bin/bash
# Create change set without executing
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --no-execute-changeset \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

echo "Change set created for review"
Change set created for review Change set name: awscli-cloudformation-package-deploy-1234567890 Status: CREATE_COMPLETE Action required: Review and execute manually

package

Packages local artifacts (Lambda code, nested templates) and uploads to S3.

Syntax
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file template.yaml \
  --s3-bucket {bucket-name} \
  --output-template-file packaged-template.yaml
1 CloudFormation package Example
Example: Package Lambda Function
#!/bin/bash
# Package template with Lambda function code
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file django-with-lambda.yaml \
  --s3-bucket anihpj-deployment-artifacts \
  --output-template-file packaged-template.yaml

# Deploy packaged template
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file packaged-template.yaml \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
Uploading to anihpj-deployment-artifacts/abc123... ✓ Lambda function code uploaded ✓ Nested templates uploaded ✓ Template transformed packaged-template.yaml created Ready for deployment

activate-organizations-access

Activates trusted access with AWS Organizations for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Enable Organizations Integration for anihpj Multi-Account Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations access for StackSets
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Verify activation
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

echo "Organizations access activated for anihpj multi-account infrastructure"
{ "Status": "ENABLED", "StatusMessage": "Organizations access activated" } Organizations access activated for anihpj multi-account infrastructure
Example 2: Setup Multi-Account Django Deployment for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for Django infrastructure across accounts
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true,RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval=false \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

# Deploy to organizational units
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-prod-123456 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2
Organizations access: ENABLED StackSet created: anihpj-django-infrastructure Deploying to OU: ou-prod-123456 Instances creating in us-east-1, us-west-2 Multi-account Django infrastructure deployment initiated
Example 3: Enable for anihpj CI/CD Pipeline Cross-Account Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for CI/CD pipeline resources
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-cicd-pipeline \
  --template-body file://cicd-resources.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=multi-account

echo "CI/CD pipeline ready for cross-account deployment"
Organizations access activated StackSet anihpj-cicd-pipeline created Auto-deployment enabled across organization CI/CD pipeline ready for cross-account deployment
Example 4: Centralized Security Configuration for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Deploy security baseline to all accounts
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-security-baseline \
  --template-body file://security-baseline.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

# Deploy to entire organization
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-security-baseline \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=r-root \
  --regions us-east-1

echo "Security baseline deployed to all anihpj accounts"
Organizations access: ACTIVE Security baseline StackSet created Deploying to root organization All accounts will receive security configuration Security baseline deployed to all anihpj accounts
Example 5: anihpj Multi-Region Django Application Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations for multi-account multi-region deployment
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for Django application
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app-global \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true,RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval=true \
  --parameters ParameterKey=ProjectName,ParameterValue=anihpj \
               ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production

# Deploy to production accounts in multiple regions
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app-global \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-prod-123456 \
  --regions us-east-1 eu-west-1 ap-southeast-1
Organizations access activated StackSet: anihpj-django-app-global Deploying to 3 regions across production accounts - us-east-1: Creating - eu-west-1: Creating - ap-southeast-1: Creating Global Django deployment initiated

activate-type

Activates a public third-party extension for use in CloudFormation operations.

Syntax
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --publisher-id {publisher-id}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Activate MongoDB Atlas Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Activate MongoDB Atlas CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --publisher-id bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082

# Verify activation
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster

echo "MongoDB Atlas extension activated for anihpj"
{ "Arn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster" } Extension activated successfully MongoDB Atlas extension activated for anihpj
Example 2: Activate Datadog Extension for anihpj Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Datadog CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311

# Set as default version
aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS

echo "Datadog monitoring extension activated for anihpj"
Extension activation initiated Type: Datadog::Integrations::AWS Status: ACTIVATED Default version set Datadog monitoring extension activated for anihpj
Example 3: Activate New Relic Extension for anihpj APM
#!/bin/bash
# Activate New Relic extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads \
  --publisher-id 759f81f13de8436c8833e4439e2b1b1a4ea71583

# List activated types
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=NewRelic \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE

echo "New Relic APM extension activated for anihpj Django monitoring"
Activating New Relic extension... Status: ACTIVATED Visibility: PUBLIC NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads ready New Relic APM extension activated for anihpj Django monitoring
Example 4: Activate PagerDuty Extension for anihpj Incident Management
#!/bin/bash
# Activate PagerDuty CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies::EscalationPolicy \
  --publisher-id 9f0b8a1d8c5e4a3f2b1c0d9e8f7a6b5c

# Configure automatic versioning
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies::EscalationPolicy \
  --configuration-alias default \
  --configuration '{"PagerDutyAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj/pagerduty:SecretString:api_key}}"}}'

echo "PagerDuty incident management activated for anihpj"
Extension activated Configuration set with Secrets Manager integration Incident management ready PagerDuty incident management activated for anihpj
Example 5: Activate GitHub Extension for anihpj Repository Management
#!/bin/bash
# Activate GitHub CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8312

# Create anihpj repository using CloudFormation
cat > github-repo.yaml << 'EOF'
Resources:
  AnihpjRepo:
    Type: GitHub::Repositories::Repository
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-django-app
      Description: anihpj Django application repository
      Private: true
      AutoInit: true
EOF

aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-github-repo \
  --template-body file://github-repo.yaml
GitHub extension activated Repository resource type available Stack creation initiated Repository: anihpj-django-app will be created Private: true Auto-initialized with README

batch-describe-type-configurations

Returns configuration data for multiple registered extensions in batch.

Syntax
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers TypeArn={arn1} TypeArn={arn2}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Extension Configurations
#!/bin/bash
# Get configurations for multiple extensions used in anihpj
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
  --output json | jq '.TypeConfigurations[] | {TypeArn, Configuration}'
{ "TypeArn": "...MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster", "Configuration": "{\"MongoDBAtlasAccess\":{\"ApiKey\":\"{{resolve:secretsmanager:...}}\"}" } { "TypeArn": "...Datadog-Integrations-AWS", "Configuration": "{\"DatadogAccess\":{\"ApiKey\":\"{{resolve:secretsmanager:...}}\"}" } All anihpj extension configurations retrieved
Example 2: Audit anihpj Third-Party Extension Settings
#!/bin/bash
# List all activated types for anihpj
TYPE_ARNS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeArn' \
  --output text)

# Batch describe configurations
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers $(echo $TYPE_ARNS | xargs -n1 echo | head -5 | xargs -I {} echo "TypeArn={}") \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[*].[TypeName,Alias,IsDefaultConfiguration]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------- | BatchDescribeTypeConfigurations | +--------------------------------+----------+------------------+ | MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster | default | True | | Datadog::Integrations::AWS | default | True | | NewRelic::Observability::... | prod | True | | PagerDuty::EscalationPo... | default | True | | GitHub::Repositories::Repo... | default | False | +--------------------------------+----------+------------------+ anihpj extension audit complete
Example 3: Verify anihpj Secrets Manager Integration
#!/bin/bash
# Check which extensions use Secrets Manager for anihpj
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[*].Configuration' \
  --output text | grep -o 'secretsmanager:[^}]*'

echo "Secrets Manager integrations for anihpj verified"
secretsmanager:anihpj/mongodb:SecretString:api_key secretsmanager:anihpj/datadog:SecretString:api_key All extensions using Secrets Manager for secure credential storage Secrets Manager integrations for anihpj verified
Example 4: Compare anihpj Dev and Prod Extension Configurations
#!/bin/bash
# Get configurations for dev environment
DEV_CONFIG=$(aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[0].Configuration' \
  --output text)

# Get configurations for prod environment  
PROD_CONFIG=$(aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:987654321098:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[0].Configuration' \
  --output text)

echo "Dev Config: $DEV_CONFIG"
echo "Prod Config: $PROD_CONFIG"
echo "anihpj environment configuration comparison complete"
Dev Config: {"MongoDBAtlasAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj-dev/mongodb}}"}} Prod Config: {"MongoDBAtlasAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj-prod/mongodb}}"}} Different secret paths for dev/prod isolation anihpj environment configuration comparison complete
Example 5: Batch Check anihpj Extension Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Get all extension configurations with version info
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/NewRelic-Observability-Aiworkloads \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeConfigurations[] | "\(.TypeName): \(.Alias // "default")"'

echo "All anihpj extension versions checked"
MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster: default Datadog::Integrations::AWS: default NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads: prod 3 extensions configured for anihpj All anihpj extension versions checked

describe-account-limits

Retrieves CloudFormation quotas and limits for your AWS account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Account CloudFormation Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Check CloudFormation limits for anihpj account
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[*].[Name,Value]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj CloudFormation account limits retrieved"
--------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeAccountLimits | +----------------------------------+--------------------+ | StackLimit | 200 | | StackSetLimit | 100 | | StackInstancesPerStackSet | 2000 | | ResourcesPerStack | 500 | | StackOutputsLimit | 200 | | ParametersPerStack | 200 | +----------------------------------+--------------------+ anihpj CloudFormation account limits retrieved
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Stack Usage Against Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Get current stack count
STACK_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'length(StackSummaries)' \
  --output text)

# Get stack limit
STACK_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackLimit`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Stacks: $STACK_COUNT / $STACK_LIMIT"
echo "Usage: $(( STACK_COUNT * 100 / STACK_LIMIT ))%"

if [ $STACK_COUNT -gt $(( STACK_LIMIT * 80 / 100 )) ]; then
  echo "WARNING: anihpj approaching stack limit!"
fi
anihpj Stacks: 45 / 200 Usage: 22% Healthy stack count for anihpj infrastructure
Example 3: Validate anihpj Django Template Against Resource Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Count resources in Django template
RESOURCE_COUNT=$(cat anihpj-django-stack.yaml | \
  yq '.Resources | length')

# Get resource limit
RESOURCE_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`ResourcesPerStack`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "Template Resources: $RESOURCE_COUNT / $RESOURCE_LIMIT"

if [ $RESOURCE_COUNT -gt $RESOURCE_LIMIT ]; then
  echo "ERROR: anihpj template exceeds resource limit!"
  echo "Consider splitting into nested stacks"
else
  echo "anihpj template within limits ✓"
fi
Template Resources: 87 / 500 Template within limits ✓ anihpj Django infrastructure template validated
Example 4: Check anihpj StackSet Capacity
#!/bin/bash
# Count existing StackSets for anihpj
STACKSET_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'length(Summaries)' \
  --output text)

# Get StackSet limit
STACKSET_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackSetLimit`].Value' \
  --output text)

# Get instance limit per StackSet
INSTANCE_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackInstancesPerStackSet`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj StackSets: $STACKSET_COUNT / $STACKSET_LIMIT"
echo "Max Instances per StackSet: $INSTANCE_LIMIT"
anihpj StackSets: 12 / 100 Max Instances per StackSet: 2000 Capacity available for additional StackSets anihpj multi-account infrastructure has room to grow
Example 5: Export anihpj Account Limits Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive limits report for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --output json > anihpj-cfn-limits.json

# Create formatted report
cat > anihpj-limits-report.txt << EOF
anihpj CloudFormation Account Limits Report
Generated: $(date)
============================================

$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[*].[Name,Value]' \
  --output text | column -t)

Current Usage:
- Stacks: $(aws cloudformation list-stacks --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE --query 'length(StackSummaries)' --output text)
- StackSets: $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'length(Summaries)' --output text)
EOF

cat anihpj-limits-report.txt
anihpj CloudFormation Account Limits Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 2026 10:30:00 ============================================ StackLimit 200 StackSetLimit 100 StackInstancesPerStackSet 2000 ResourcesPerStack 500 StackOutputsLimit 200 ParametersPerStack 200 Current Usage: - Stacks: 45 - StackSets: 12 Report saved to anihpj-limits-report.txt

describe-change-set-hooks

Returns information about hooks associated with a change set.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --change-set-name {change-set-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check Security Hooks on anihpj Django Stack Change Set
#!/bin/bash
# Describe hooks for anihpj stack change set
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name anihpj-update-2026-01 \
  --query 'Hooks[*].[HookType,TargetType,InvocationPoint,Status]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj change set hooks reviewed"
----------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSetHooks | +--------------------------------+--------+---------------+-----------+ | AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | AWS::GuardDuty::Validator | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | Custom::CostValidator | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | WARNING | +--------------------------------+--------+---------------+-----------+ anihpj change set hooks reviewed
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Compliance Hook Results
#!/bin/bash
# Check compliance hooks for anihpj infrastructure update
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --change-set-name add-rds-encryption \
  --output json | jq -r '.Hooks[] | select(.HookType | contains("Compliance")) | 
{
  Type: .HookType,
  Status: .Status,
  StatusMessage: .StatusMessage,
  TargetResources: (.TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails // [] | map(.LogicalResourceId))
}'
{ "Type": "AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance", "Status": "SUCCESS", "StatusMessage": "All security requirements met", "TargetResources": [ "AnihpjDatabase", "AnihpjDBSubnetGroup" ] } Compliance hooks passed for anihpj RDS encryption update
Example 3: Debug Failed Hook for anihpj Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed hook in anihpj change set
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name security-update \
  --query 'Hooks[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | 
"Hook: \(.HookType)
Status: \(.Status)
Failure Mode: \(.FailureMode)
Message: \(.StatusMessage)
Target: \(.TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails[].LogicalResourceId)
"'

echo "anihpj hook failure analyzed"
Hook: Custom::SecurityValidator Status: FAILED Failure Mode: FAIL Message: Security group allows 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on port 22 Target: AnihpjSecurityGroup Change set will not proceed due to FAIL mode anihpj hook failure analyzed - Fix required before deployment
Example 4: Review anihpj Cost Validation Hooks
#!/bin/bash
# Check cost validation hooks for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --change-set-name scale-up-instances \
  --query 'Hooks[?contains(HookType, `Cost`)].[HookType,Status,StatusMessage]' \
  --output table

# Get hook invocation details
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --change-set-name scale-up-instances \
  --query 'Hooks[?contains(HookType, `Cost`)].TargetDetails' \
  --output json | jq '.'
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSetHooks | +---------------------------+---------+----------------------------+ | Custom::CostValidator | WARNING | Estimated cost: $450/month | +---------------------------+---------+----------------------------+ Target Resources: - AnihpjEC2Instance (t3.micro → t3.large) - AnihpjRDSInstance (db.t3.micro → db.t3.medium) Cost increase acceptable for anihpj production scaling
Example 5: List All Hook Types for anihpj Change Set
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive hook report for anihpj
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
CHANGE_SET_NAME="major-update-2026"

echo "anihpj Change Set Hook Report"
echo "=============================="

aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
  --output json | jq -r '
.Hooks | group_by(.InvocationPoint) | .[] | 
"
\(.[0].InvocationPoint) Hooks:
\(map("  • \(.HookType): \(.Status)") | join("\n"))
"'

# Count hooks by status
echo "Hook Status Summary:"
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
  --query 'Hooks[*].Status' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c
anihpj Change Set Hook Report ============================== PRE_PROVISION Hooks: • AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance: SUCCESS • AWS::GuardDuty::Validator: SUCCESS • Custom::CostValidator: WARNING • Custom::TagValidator: SUCCESS POST_PROVISION Hooks: • Custom::NotificationHook: SUCCESS Hook Status Summary: 4 SUCCESS 1 WARNING anihpj change set hooks validated

deactivate-organizations-access

Deactivates trusted access with AWS Organizations for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Disable Organizations Access for anihpj Maintenance
#!/bin/bash
# Check current status
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

# Deactivate Organizations access temporarily
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Verify deactivation
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

echo "anihpj Organizations access deactivated for maintenance"
Current Status: ENABLED Deactivating Organizations access... Status: DISABLED anihpj Organizations access deactivated for maintenance
Example 2: Remove anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Capability
#!/bin/bash
# List existing StackSets with SERVICE_MANAGED permissions
echo "Existing anihpj StackSets:"
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`].StackSetName' \
  --output table

# Deactivate Organizations access
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

echo "anihpj multi-account deployment disabled"
echo "Note: Existing StackSets remain but cannot be updated"
Existing anihpj StackSets: ------------------------------ | anihpj-django-infrastructure | | anihpj-security-baseline | | anihpj-cicd-pipeline | ------------------------------ Organizations access deactivated anihpj multi-account deployment disabled Note: Existing StackSets remain but cannot be updated
Example 3: Deactivate for anihpj Security Audit
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate during security audit
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Document current StackSet deployments
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE > anihpj-stacksets-backup.json

# List all instances
for STACKSET in $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'Summaries[].StackSetName' --output text); do
  echo "StackSet: $STACKSET"
  aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status]' \
    --output table
done

echo "anihpj Organizations access disabled for security audit"
Organizations access: DISABLED Backing up StackSet configurations... StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure ------------------------------------------- | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | | 123456789012 | us-west-2 | CURRENT | ------------------------------------------- anihpj Organizations access disabled for security audit
Example 4: Temporary Deactivation for anihpj Account Migration
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate before account migration
echo "Deactivating Organizations access for anihpj account migration..."
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Wait for any in-progress operations to complete
echo "Checking for in-progress StackSet operations..."
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj ready for account migration"
echo "Re-activate with: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
Deactivating Organizations access for anihpj account migration... Status: DISABLED Checking for in-progress StackSet operations... No running operations anihpj ready for account migration Re-activate with: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access
Example 5: Deactivate and Switch to Self-Managed StackSets for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate Organizations access
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Create new self-managed StackSet for anihpj
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-selfmanaged \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --permission-model SELF_MANAGED \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

# Deploy using explicit account IDs
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-selfmanaged \
  --accounts 123456789012 987654321098 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2

echo "anihpj switched to self-managed StackSets"
Organizations access deactivated Creating self-managed StackSet: anihpj-django-selfmanaged Permission model: SELF_MANAGED Requires AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole in target accounts Stack instances creating... anihpj switched to self-managed StackSets

deactivate-type

Deactivates a public or private extension in your account and region.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Deactivate Unused MongoDB Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Check if MongoDB extension is in use
echo "Checking anihpj stacks using MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster..."
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=MongoDB

# Deactivate MongoDB extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster

echo "MongoDB extension deactivated for anihpj account"
Checking stacks using MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster... No active stacks found Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED MongoDB extension deactivated for anihpj account
Example 2: Remove Third-Party Extension from anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# List all activated third-party extensions
echo "anihpj Activated Extensions:"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,IsActivated]' \
  --output table

# Deactivate Datadog extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS

echo "Datadog extension removed from anihpj"
anihpj Activated Extensions: --------------------------------------------------- | MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster | True | | Datadog::Integrations::AWS | True | | NewRelic::Observability::... | True | --------------------------------------------------- Deactivating Datadog::Integrations::AWS... Extension deactivated Datadog extension removed from anihpj
Example 3: Deactivate All Versions of Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# List all versions of GitHub extension
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,IsDefaultVersion]' \
  --output table

# Deactivate the extension (all versions)
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository

echo "All versions of GitHub extension deactivated for anihpj"
GitHub Extension Versions: --------------------------------- | 00000001 | True | | 00000002 | False | --------------------------------- Deactivating GitHub::Repositories::Repository... All versions deactivated All versions of GitHub extension deactivated for anihpj
Example 4: Clean Up anihpj Test Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# List private extensions for anihpj
echo "anihpj Private Extensions:"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text

# Deactivate test extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::TestResource

# Deregister to completely remove
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::TestResource

echo "Test extension removed from anihpj"
anihpj Private Extensions: anihpj::Custom::TestResource anihpj::Custom::DjangoResource Deactivating test extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Deregistering extension... Extension removed Test extension removed from anihpj
Example 5: Deactivate Extension Before Cost Optimization for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Check extension usage in anihpj
EXTENSION="NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads"

echo "Checking $EXTENSION usage in anihpj stacks..."
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=NewRelic

# No usage found, deactivate to reduce costs
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $EXTENSION

# Verify deactivation
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $EXTENSION 2>&1 || echo "Extension fully deactivated"

echo "anihpj cost optimization: $EXTENSION removed"
Checking NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads usage in anihpj stacks... Extension found but not used in any active stacks Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Extension fully deactivated anihpj cost optimization: NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads removed

deregister-type

Removes a registered private extension from the CloudFormation registry.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Deregister anihpj Custom Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# List anihpj private extensions
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[?contains(TypeName, `anihpj`)].TypeName' \
  --output table

# Deregister custom Django resource
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application

echo "anihpj custom resource deregistered"
anihpj Private Extensions: --------------------------------- | anihpj::Django::Application | | anihpj::Custom::Database | --------------------------------- Deregistering anihpj::Django::Application... Type removed from registry anihpj custom resource deregistered
Example 2: Remove Old Version of anihpj Custom Extension
#!/bin/bash
# List all versions of anihpj custom type
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::DjangoDeployment \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,TimeCreated,IsDefaultVersion]' \
  --output table

# Deregister old version
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::DjangoDeployment \
  --version-id 00000001

echo "Old version of anihpj custom extension removed"
Extension Versions: ------------------------------------------------ | 00000001 | 2025-11-15 | False | | 00000002 | 2026-01-10 | True | ------------------------------------------------ Deregistering version 00000001... Version removed Old version of anihpj custom extension removed Latest version (00000002) remains active
Example 3: Clean Up anihpj Test Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Find all anihpj test extensions
TEST_TYPES=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[?contains(TypeName, `Test`)].TypeName' \
  --output text)

# Deregister each test type
for TYPE in $TEST_TYPES; do
  echo "Deregistering $TYPE..."
  aws cloudformation deregister-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE
done

echo "All anihpj test extensions cleaned up"
Deregistering anihpj::Test::DjangoResource... Type removed Deregistering anihpj::Test::DatabaseMigration... Type removed Deregistering anihpj::Test::CacheConfig... Type removed All anihpj test extensions cleaned up
Example 4: Deregister anihpj Hook Handler
#!/bin/bash
# List anihpj hooks
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type HOOK \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,Description]' \
  --output table

# Deregister security validation hook
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name anihpj::Security::ValidationHook

echo "anihpj security hook deregistered"
anihpj Hooks: --------------------------------------------------------- | anihpj::Security::ValidationHook | Validates security | | anihpj::Cost::EstimationHook | Estimates costs | --------------------------------------------------------- Deregistering anihpj::Security::ValidationHook... Hook removed from registry anihpj security hook deregistered
Example 5: Complete Cleanup of anihpj Private Extension
#!/bin/bash
EXTENSION="anihpj::Django::CustomResource"

# Check if extension is in use
echo "Checking if $EXTENSION is used in any stacks..."
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[].StackName' \
  --output text)

IN_USE=false
for STACK in $STACKS; do
  if aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query "StackResources[?ResourceType=='$EXTENSION']" \
    --output text | grep -q .; then
    echo "WARNING: $EXTENSION in use by $STACK"
    IN_USE=true
  fi
done

if [ "$IN_USE" = false ]; then
  # Deactivate first
  aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION
  
  # Then deregister
  aws cloudformation deregister-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION
  
  echo "$EXTENSION completely removed from anihpj"
else
  echo "Cannot deregister - extension in use"
fi
Checking if anihpj::Django::CustomResource is used in any stacks... No active usage found Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Deregistering extension... Type removed from registry anihpj::Django::CustomResource completely removed from anihpj

describe-events

Returns all stack and resource events for a stack. Alias for describe-stack-events.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: View Recent Events for anihpj Django Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Get recent events for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 10 \
  --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeEvents | +-------------------------+------------------+----------------------+----------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AWS::CloudFormation | anihpj-... | | 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AWS::RDS::DBInstance| AnihpjDB | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | UPDATE_IN_PROG..| AWS::RDS::DBInstance| AnihpjDB | | 2026-01-17T10:27:00Z | UPDATE_IN_PROG..| AWS::CloudFormation | anihpj-... | +-------------------------+------------------+----------------------+----------------+ anihpj stack update events
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Stack Creation Events
#!/bin/bash
# Watch stack creation events in real-time
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

while true; do
  clear
  echo "anihpj Stack Events - $(date)"
  echo "================================"
  
  aws cloudformation describe-events \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --max-items 15 \
    --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
    --output table
  
  # Check if stack creation complete
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  if [[ $STATUS == "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]] || [[ $STATUS == *"FAILED"* ]]; then
    echo "Final Status: $STATUS"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
anihpj Stack Events - Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 ================================ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | anihpj-django-production | | | 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjDatabase | Resource creation initiated | Final Status: CREATE_COMPLETE
Example 3: Find Failed Resources in anihpj Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Find all failed events for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceStatus, `FAILED`)].[Timestamp,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "anihpj Stack Failures Summary:"
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceStatus, `FAILED`)] | length(@)' \
  --output text | xargs -I {} echo "Total failures: {}"
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeEvents | +----------------------+--------------------+------------------+-------------------------+ | 2026-01-17T09:15:00Z | AWS::EC2::Security | AnihpjSG | Invalid CIDR block | | 2026-01-17T08:45:00Z | AWS::RDS::DBInst.. | AnihpjDB | Insufficient storage | +----------------------+--------------------+------------------+-------------------------+ anihpj Stack Failures Summary: Total failures: 2
Example 4: Filter anihpj Events by Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# Show only RDS events for anihpj database
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceType==`AWS::RDS::DBInstance`].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "EC2 Instance Events:"
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceType==`AWS::EC2::Instance`].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output table
RDS Events: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjDatabase | Requested update | | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ EC2 Instance Events: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjWebServer | | | 2026-01-17T10:14:00Z | UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjWebServer | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Example 5: Export anihpj Stack Events to File
#!/bin/bash
# Export all events for anihpj stack to JSON
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json > $OUTPUT_FILE

# Create readable report
echo "anihpj Stack Events Report" > anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME" >> anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "================================" >> anihpj-events-report.txt

aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output text >> anihpj-events-report.txt

echo "Events exported to $OUTPUT_FILE and anihpj-events-report.txt"
Events exported to anihpj-stack-events-20260117.json and anihpj-events-report.txt anihpj Stack Events Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 Stack: anihpj-django-app ================================ 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z UPDATE_COMPLETE anihpj-django-app 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z UPDATE_COMPLETE AnihpjDatabase ... Total events logged: 47

describe-generated-template

Describes a generated template including its status and resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {template-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check Status of anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Describe generated template for anihpj infrastructure
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-import \
  --query '{Name:GeneratedTemplateName,Status:Status,ResourceCount:TotalResourcesCount,CreatedTime:CreationTime}' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeGeneratedTemplate | +----------------+--------------------+------------------------+ | CreatedTime | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | | Name | anihpj-django-import | | ResourceCount | 15 | | Status | COMPLETE | +----------------+--------------------+------------------------+ anihpj generated template ready for use
Example 2: View Resources in anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed resource information
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-infrastructure-import \
  --query 'Resources[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Template Statistics:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-infrastructure-import \
  --query '{Total:TotalResourcesCount,Pending:PendingResourcesCount,Complete:CompleteResourcesCount}' \
  --output json | jq '.'
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeGeneratedTemplate | +-------------------+----------------------+-----------------------------+ | AnihpjVPC | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-abc123 | | AnihpjSubnet1 | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-def456 | | AnihpjDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db | | AnihpjInstance | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0123456789abcdef0 | +-------------------+----------------------+-----------------------------+ Template Statistics: { "Total": 15, "Pending": 0, "Complete": 15 } anihpj infrastructure template generation complete
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Template Generation Progress
#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-django-migration"

echo "Monitoring generation of $TEMPLATE_NAME..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
    --query '{Status:Status,Total:TotalResourcesCount,Complete:CompleteResourcesCount,Pending:PendingResourcesCount}' \
    --output json)
  
  echo "$(date): $STATUS"
  
  STATUS_TEXT=$(echo $STATUS | jq -r '.Status')
  if [[ $STATUS_TEXT == "COMPLETE" ]] || [[ $STATUS_TEXT == "FAILED" ]]; then
    echo "Template generation finished: $STATUS_TEXT"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 5
done

echo "anihpj template ready"
Monitoring generation of anihpj-django-migration... Fri Jan 17 10:25:00 2026: {"Status":"IN_PROGRESS","Total":15,"Complete":8,"Pending":7} Fri Jan 17 10:25:05 2026: {"Status":"IN_PROGRESS","Total":15,"Complete":12,"Pending":3} Fri Jan 17 10:25:10 2026: {"Status":"COMPLETE","Total":15,"Complete":15,"Pending":0} Template generation finished: COMPLETE anihpj template ready
Example 4: View anihpj Template Generation Warnings
#!/bin/bash
# Check for warnings or issues in generated template
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-import \
  --output json | jq '{
    Name: .GeneratedTemplateName,
    Status: .Status,
    Warnings: .StatusReason,
    Resources: .Resources[] | select(.Warnings != null) | {
      LogicalId: .LogicalResourceId,
      Type: .ResourceType,
      Warnings: .Warnings
    }
  }'

echo "anihpj template warnings reviewed"
{ "Name": "anihpj-django-import", "Status": "COMPLETE", "Warnings": null, "Resources": { "LogicalId": "AnihpjSecurityGroup", "Type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "Warnings": "Some properties could not be imported automatically" } } anihpj template warnings reviewed Manual review recommended for AnihpjSecurityGroup
Example 5: Export anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get generated template details
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

# Describe the template
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --output json > anihpj-template-description.json

# Get the actual template body
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --format YAML > anihpj-generated-template.yaml

echo "anihpj Generated Template Exported"
echo "=================================="
echo "Description: anihpj-template-description.json"
echo "Template: anihpj-generated-template.yaml"
echo ""
echo "Resources:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query 'Resources[*].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | nl
anihpj Generated Template Exported ================================== Description: anihpj-template-description.json Template: anihpj-generated-template.yaml Resources: 1 AnihpjVPC 2 AnihpjSubnet1 3 AnihpjSubnet2 4 AnihpjRouteTable 5 AnihpjSecurityGroup 6 AnihpjDatabase 7 AnihpjInstance 8 AnihpjLoadBalancer 9 AnihpjTargetGroup 10 AnihpjListener 11 AnihpjLaunchTemplate 12 AnihpjAutoScalingGroup 13 AnihpjCacheCluster 14 AnihpjS3Bucket 15 AnihpjIAMRole Template ready for deployment

describe-organizations-access

Retrieves the current status of Organizations access for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Organizations Integration Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check if Organizations access is enabled for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
  --query '{Status:Status,StatusMessage:StatusMessage}' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj Organizations access status checked"
---------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeOrganizationsAccess | +---------------+----------------------------------------+ | Status | ENABLED | | StatusMessage | Organizations access is active | +---------------+----------------------------------------+ anihpj Organizations access status checked
Example 2: Verify anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Capability
#!/bin/bash
# Check Organizations access before creating StackSet
ORG_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

if [ "$ORG_STATUS" == "ENABLED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Organizations access enabled for anihpj"
  echo "✓ Ready for multi-account StackSet deployments"
  
  # List existing StackSets
  echo ""
  echo "Existing anihpj StackSets:"
  aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
    --status ACTIVE \
    --query 'Summaries[*].[StackSetName,PermissionModel]' \
    --output table
else
  echo "✗ Organizations access not enabled"
  echo "Run: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
fi
✓ Organizations access enabled for anihpj ✓ Ready for multi-account StackSet deployments Existing anihpj StackSets: --------------------------------------------------------- | anihpj-django-infrastructure | SERVICE_MANAGED | | anihpj-security-baseline | SERVICE_MANAGED | | anihpj-cicd-pipeline | SERVICE_MANAGED | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj multi-account capability verified
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Organizations Access During Maintenance
#!/bin/bash
# Continuous monitoring of Organizations access
echo "Monitoring anihpj Organizations access..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
    --query 'Status' \
    --output text)
  
  TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
  echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Organizations Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" != "ENABLED" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: anihpj Organizations access is $STATUS"
    # Send notification
  fi
  
  sleep 60
done
Monitoring anihpj Organizations access... [2026-01-17 10:30:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED [2026-01-17 10:31:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED [2026-01-17 10:32:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED anihpj Organizations access stable
Example 4: anihpj Pre-Deployment Organizations Check
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive pre-deployment check for anihpj
echo "anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Pre-Check"
echo "=========================================="

# Check Organizations access
ORG_ACCESS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access --output json)
echo "$ORG_ACCESS" | jq '{Status, StatusMessage}'

# Check if we're in Organizations management account
ORG_INFO=$(aws organizations describe-organization 2>/dev/null)
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Organization Details:"
  echo "$ORG_INFO" | jq '{Id:.Organization.Id,MasterAccountId:.Organization.MasterAccountId}'
else
  echo "Not in Organizations management account"
fi

# Check StackSet limits
echo ""
echo "StackSet Capacity:"
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackSetLimit` || Name==`StackInstancesPerStackSet`].[Name,Value]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj ready for multi-account deployment"
anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Pre-Check ========================================== { "Status": "ENABLED", "StatusMessage": "Organizations access is active" } Organization Details: { "Id": "o-abc123def456", "MasterAccountId": "123456789012" } StackSet Capacity: --------------------------------------------------------- | StackSetLimit | 100 | | StackInstancesPerStackSet | 2000 | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj ready for multi-account deployment
Example 5: anihpj Organizations Access Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Complete health check for anihpj multi-account setup
echo "anihpj Organizations Health Check"
echo "================================="

# Organizations access status
ORG_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access --query 'Status' --output text)
echo "Organizations Access: $ORG_STATUS"

# Check trust relationship
if [ "$ORG_STATUS" == "ENABLED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Organizations trust established"
  
  # Count StackSets using Organizations
  STACKSET_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
    --status ACTIVE \
    --query 'length(Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`])' \
    --output text)
  echo "✓ SERVICE_MANAGED StackSets: $STACKSET_COUNT"
  
  # Count total stack instances across organization
  TOTAL_INSTANCES=0
  for SS in $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`].StackSetName' --output text); do
    COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instances --stack-set-name $SS --query 'length(Summaries)' --output text)
    TOTAL_INSTANCES=$((TOTAL_INSTANCES + COUNT))
  done
  echo "✓ Total Stack Instances: $TOTAL_INSTANCES"
  
  echo ""
  echo "anihpj multi-account infrastructure healthy"
else
  echo "✗ Organizations access not enabled"
  echo "Run: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
fi
anihpj Organizations Health Check ================================= Organizations Access: ENABLED ✓ Organizations trust established ✓ SERVICE_MANAGED StackSets: 3 ✓ Total Stack Instances: 12 anihpj multi-account infrastructure healthy

describe-publisher

Returns information about a CloudFormation extension publisher.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id {publisher-id}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check MongoDB Atlas Publisher for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get MongoDB Atlas publisher information
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 \
  --query '{PublisherId:PublisherId,PublisherName:PublisherName,IdentityProvider:IdentityProvider,PublisherStatus:PublisherStatus}' \
  --output table

echo "Publisher verified for anihpj MongoDB integration"
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribePublisher | +--------------------+------------------------------------------------+ | IdentityProvider | AWS_Marketplace | | PublisherId | bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 | | PublisherName | MongoDB | | PublisherStatus | VERIFIED | +--------------------+------------------------------------------------+ Publisher verified for anihpj MongoDB integration
Example 2: Verify Datadog Publisher for anihpj Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Check Datadog publisher details
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311 \
  --output json | jq '{
    Name: .PublisherName,
    Status: .PublisherStatus,
    Verified: (.PublisherStatus == "VERIFIED"),
    Provider: .IdentityProvider
  }'

echo "anihpj monitoring extension publisher verified"
{ "Name": "Datadog", "Status": "VERIFIED", "Verified": true, "Provider": "AWS_Marketplace" } anihpj monitoring extension publisher verified Safe to use Datadog extensions
Example 3: List All Publishers Used by anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get all activated extensions and their publishers
echo "anihpj Extension Publishers"
echo "============================"

aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,PublisherId]' \
  --output text | while read TYPE_NAME PUB_ID; do
  
  if [ -n "$PUB_ID" ]; then
    PUB_NAME=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
      --publisher-id $PUB_ID \
      --query 'PublisherName' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
    
    echo "$TYPE_NAME -> $PUB_NAME ($PUB_ID)"
  fi
done
anihpj Extension Publishers ============================ MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster -> MongoDB (bb989456...) Datadog::Integrations::AWS -> Datadog (40898865...) NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads -> New Relic (759f81f1...) PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies -> PagerDuty (9f0b8a1d...) All publishers verified for anihpj
Example 4: Validate Publisher Before Activating Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Validate publisher before using their extension
PUBLISHER_ID="bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082"
EXTENSION_TYPE="MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster"

echo "Validating publisher for anihpj..."

PUB_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id $PUBLISHER_ID \
  --output json)

PUB_STATUS=$(echo "$PUB_INFO" | jq -r '.PublisherStatus')
PUB_NAME=$(echo "$PUB_INFO" | jq -r '.PublisherName')

echo "Publisher: $PUB_NAME"
echo "Status: $PUB_STATUS"

if [ "$PUB_STATUS" == "VERIFIED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Publisher verified by AWS"
  echo "Activating $EXTENSION_TYPE for anihpj..."
  
  aws cloudformation activate-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION_TYPE \
    --publisher-id $PUBLISHER_ID
  
  echo "Extension activated for anihpj"
else
  echo "✗ Publisher not verified"
  echo "Consider security implications before proceeding"
fi
Validating publisher for anihpj... Publisher: MongoDB Status: VERIFIED ✓ Publisher verified by AWS Activating MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster for anihpj... Extension activation initiated Extension activated for anihpj
Example 5: Generate anihpj Publisher Trust Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create comprehensive publisher report for anihpj
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-publisher-report.txt"

echo "anihpj CloudFormation Extension Publisher Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "=================================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Get all unique publisher IDs from activated extensions
PUBLISHERS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].PublisherId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort -u)

for PUB_ID in $PUBLISHERS; do
  if [ -n "$PUB_ID" ]; then
    echo "Publisher ID: $PUB_ID" >> $REPORT_FILE
    aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
      --publisher-id $PUB_ID \
      --query '{Name:PublisherName,Status:PublisherStatus,Provider:IdentityProvider}' \
      --output text >> $REPORT_FILE
    echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
  fi
done

cat $REPORT_FILE
echo ""
echo "Report saved to $REPORT_FILE"
anihpj CloudFormation Extension Publisher Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 ================================================= Publisher ID: bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 MongoDB VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Publisher ID: 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311 Datadog VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Publisher ID: 759f81f13de8436c8833e4439e2b1b1a4ea71583 New Relic VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Report saved to anihpj-publisher-report.txt

describe-resource-scan

Describes a resource scan that detects resources in your account not managed by CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id {scan-id}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Resource Scan Status
#!/bin/bash
# Start resource scan for anihpj account
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan \
  --query 'ResourceScanId' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan ID: $SCAN_ID"

# Monitor scan progress
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query '{ScanId:ResourceScanId,Status:Status,Progress:PercentageCompleted,StartTime:StartTime}' \
  --output table
anihpj Resource Scan ID: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123 ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeResourceScan | +----------------------+----------------------------------------------+ | PercentageCompleted | 35 | | ScanId | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/... | | StartTime | 2026-01-17T10:25:00Z | | Status | IN_PROGRESS | +----------------------+----------------------------------------------+ anihpj resource discovery in progress
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Resource Scan Completion
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj resource scan..."

while true; do
  SCAN_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.Status')
  PROGRESS=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.PercentageCompleted // 0')
  RESOURCES=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.ResourcesScanned // 0')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS | Progress: $PROGRESS% | Resources: $RESOURCES"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq '{Status,ResourcesScanned,ResourceTypes,EndTime}'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done

echo "anihpj resource scan finished"
Monitoring anihpj resource scan... 10:25:00 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 25% | Resources: 45 10:25:10 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 50% | Resources: 92 10:25:20 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 75% | Resources: 138 10:25:30 - Status: COMPLETE | Progress: 100% | Resources: 187 { "Status": "COMPLETE", "ResourcesScanned": 187, "ResourceTypes": 23, "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:25:35Z" } anihpj resource scan finished
Example 3: Find Unmanaged anihpj Django Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Scan for unmanaged resources
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan --query 'ResourceScanId' --output text)

# Wait for completion
echo "Scanning for unmanaged anihpj resources..."
aws cloudformation wait resource-scan-complete --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID 2>/dev/null

# Get scan results
SCAN_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan Results:"
echo "$SCAN_INFO" | jq '{
  TotalResources: .ResourcesScanned,
  ResourceTypes: .ResourceTypes,
  Status: .Status,
  Duration: (.EndTime - .StartTime)
}'

# List discovered resources
echo ""
echo "Sample unmanaged resources:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --max-results 10 \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table
Scanning for unmanaged anihpj resources... anihpj Resource Scan Results: { "TotalResources": 187, "ResourceTypes": 23, "Status": "COMPLETE", "Duration": "PT5M30S" } Sample unmanaged resources: --------------------------------------------------------------- | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0abc123def456789 | | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-legacy-db | | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-uploads-bucket | | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-manual-created | | AWS::DynamoDB::Table | anihpj-sessions | --------------------------------------------------------------- Consider importing these into CloudFormation stacks
Example 4: Check anihpj Scan for Specific Resource Types
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"

# Get scan details
SCAN_DETAILS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan Analysis"
echo "=============================="
echo "$SCAN_DETAILS" | jq '{
  ScanId: .ResourceScanId,
  Status: .Status,
  TotalResources: .ResourcesScanned,
  ResourceTypeCount: .ResourceTypes,
  StartTime: .StartTime,
  EndTime: .EndTime
}'

# Count resources by type
echo ""
echo "Resources by Type:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].ResourceType' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

echo ""
echo "Use list-resource-scan-resources to see all anihpj resources"
anihpj Resource Scan Analysis ============================== { "ScanId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123", "Status": "COMPLETE", "TotalResources": 187, "ResourceTypeCount": 23, "StartTime": "2026-01-17T10:25:00Z", "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:30:35Z" } Resources by Type: 45 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 23 AWS::EC2::Instance 18 AWS::S3::Bucket 15 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 12 AWS::Lambda::Function 10 AWS::DynamoDB::Table 8 AWS::EC2::VPC 7 AWS::IAM::Role 6 AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster 5 AWS::ECS::Cluster Use list-resource-scan-resources to see all anihpj resources
Example 5: Export anihpj Resource Scan Report
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-resource-scan-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json"

# Get complete scan details
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json > $REPORT_FILE

# Create summary report
cat > anihpj-scan-summary.txt << EOF
anihpj CloudFormation Resource Scan Summary
Generated: $(date)
==========================================

Scan ID: $SCAN_ID
Status: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'Status' --output text)
Total Resources: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'ResourcesScanned' --output text)
Resource Types: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'ResourceTypes' --output text)

Top Resource Types:
$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'Resources[*].ResourceType' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5)

Next Steps:
1. Review unmanaged resources
2. Create generated templates for import
3. Import resources into CloudFormation stacks
EOF

cat anihpj-scan-summary.txt
echo ""
echo "Full scan data saved to $REPORT_FILE"
anihpj CloudFormation Resource Scan Summary Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:35:00 2026 ========================================== Scan ID: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123 Status: COMPLETE Total Resources: 187 Resource Types: 23 Top Resource Types: 45 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 23 AWS::EC2::Instance 18 AWS::S3::Bucket 15 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 12 AWS::Lambda::Function Next Steps: 1. Review unmanaged resources 2. Create generated templates for import 3. Import resources into CloudFormation stacks Full scan data saved to anihpj-resource-scan-20260117-103500.json

describe-stack-instance

Returns detailed information about a stack instance in a StackSet.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --stack-instance-account {account-id} \
  --stack-instance-region {region}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Stack Instance Status
#!/bin/bash
# Describe specific anihpj stack instance
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --stack-instance-region us-east-1 \
  --query 'StackInstance.{Account:Account,Region:Region,Status:Status,StatusReason:StatusReason}' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackInstance | +--------------+-----------------+------------+-----------------+ | Account | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | | Region | us-east-1 | | | | Status | CURRENT | | | | StatusReason | null | | | +--------------+-----------------+------------+-----------------+ anihpj stack instance healthy
Example 2: Get anihpj Stack Instance Drift Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check drift detection status for anihpj instance
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --stack-instance-region us-west-2 \
  --output json | jq '{
    StackId: .StackInstance.StackId,
    Status: .StackInstance.Status,
    DriftStatus: .StackInstance.DriftStatus,
    LastDriftCheck: .StackInstance.LastDriftCheckTimestamp,
    ParameterOverrides: .StackInstance.ParameterOverrides
  }'
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-west-2:123456789012:stack/...", "Status": "CURRENT", "DriftStatus": "IN_SYNC", "LastDriftCheck": "2026-01-17T09:00:00Z", "ParameterOverrides": [ { "ParameterKey": "Environment", "ParameterValue": "production" } ] } anihpj stack instance synchronized with StackSet
Example 3: Review anihpj Parameter Overrides for Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Get parameter overrides for anihpj production instance
STACK_SET="anihpj-django-app"
ACCOUNT="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --query 'StackInstance.ParameterOverrides[*].[ParameterKey,ParameterValue]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Base StackSet parameters:"
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --query 'StackSet.Parameters[*].[ParameterKey,ParameterValue]' \
  --output table
Instance Parameter Overrides: --------------------------------------------------------- | Environment | production | | InstanceType | t3.large | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.medium | --------------------------------------------------------- Base StackSet parameters: --------------------------------------------------------- | Environment | dev | | InstanceType | t3.micro | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.micro | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj production instance uses custom parameters
Example 4: Check All anihpj Stack Instances in Region
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj instances in specific region
STACK_SET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "anihpj Stack Instances in $REGION:"
echo "==================================="

# Get all accounts with instances in region
ACCOUNTS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
