# Step 3: Verify status
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Checking verification status..."
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --query 'PublisherStatus' --output text)
echo "  Publisher Status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" != "VERIFIED" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  Next steps to verify:"
  echo "  1. Check email for verification link"
  echo "  2. Complete identity verification"
  echo "  3. Wait for approval (1-2 business days)"
fi

echo ""
echo "anihpj publisher setup complete"
anihpj CloudFormation Publisher Setup ======================================= Step 1: Setting up GitHub connection... Manual step: Create connection in AWS Console Navigate to: Developer Tools > Settings > Connections Step 2: Registering as publisher... ✓ Registered with ID: 408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1 Step 3: Checking verification status... Publisher Status: VERIFIED anihpj publisher setup complete
Example 5: anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check
#!/bin/bash
# Check prerequisites before registering
echo "anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check"
echo "======================================"

# Check AWS account
echo "AWS Account: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Account' --output text)"

# Check IAM permissions
echo ""
echo "Checking IAM permissions..."
REQUIRED_ACTIONS=(
  "cloudformation:RegisterPublisher"
  "cloudformation:DescribePublisher"
  "cloudformation:RegisterType"
  "cloudformation:PublishType"
)

for ACTION in "${REQUIRED_ACTIONS[@]}"; do
  # Simplified check - in practice use IAM policy simulator
  echo "  - $ACTION"
done

# Check existing registration
echo ""
echo "Existing Registration:"
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --output json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '{
    Status: .PublisherStatus,
    Id: .PublisherId
  }' || echo "  Not registered"

echo ""
echo "To register: aws cloudformation register-publisher --accept-terms-and-conditions"
anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check ====================================== AWS Account: 123456789012 Checking IAM permissions... - cloudformation:RegisterPublisher - cloudformation:DescribePublisher - cloudformation:RegisterType - cloudformation:PublishType Existing Registration: { "Status": "VERIFIED", "Id": "408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1" } Ready to publish anihpj extensions

register-type

Registers an extension as a resource type, hook, or module in CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --schema-handler-package {s3-url}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Register anihpj Custom Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# Register anihpj Django application resource type
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "anihpj::Django::Application" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application.zip"

echo "anihpj custom resource type registration initiated"
{ "RegistrationToken": "reg-token-abc123-def456-ghi789" } anihpj custom resource type registration initiated Use describe-type-registration to check status
Example 2: Register anihpj with Execution Role
#!/bin/bash
# Register with specific execution role
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
S3_PACKAGE="s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application-v3.zip"
EXEC_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cfn-extension-role"

echo "Registering anihpj extension..."

RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
  --schema-handler-package "$S3_PACKAGE" \
  --execution-role-arn "$EXEC_ROLE" \
  --output json)

TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
echo "Registration Token: $TOKEN"

# Wait for registration
echo "Waiting for registration to complete..."
for i in {1..30}; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
    --query 'ProgressStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "  Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "anihpj extension registered successfully!"
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo ""
    aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
      --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
      --query 'StatusMessage' --output text
    exit 1
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
Registering anihpj extension... Registration Token: reg-token-abc123-def456-ghi789 Waiting for registration to complete... Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: COMPLETE anihpj extension registered successfully!
Example 3: Register anihpj Security Hook
#!/bin/bash
# Register anihpj security validation hook
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name "anihpj::Security::ValidationHook" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-security-hook.zip" \
  --logging-config LogRoleArn=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-hook-logging,LogGroupName=/aws/cloudformation/hooks/anihpj

echo "anihpj security hook registration started"
{ "RegistrationToken": "reg-token-hook-xyz789" } anihpj security hook registration started
Example 4: Complete anihpj Extension Registration Workflow
#!/bin/bash
# Full registration workflow for anihpj extension
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
VERSION="3.0"

echo "anihpj Extension Registration Workflow"
echo "========================================"

# Step 1: Build and package
echo "Step 1: Building extension package..."
cd /path/to/anihpj-extension
cfn generate
cfn submit --dry-run
echo "  ✓ Package built: anihpj-django-application.zip"

# Step 2: Upload to S3
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Uploading to S3..."
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extensions"
S3_KEY="anihpj-django-application-v${VERSION}.zip"
aws s3 cp anihpj-django-application.zip "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}"
echo "  ✓ Uploaded to s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}"

# Step 3: Register type
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Registering type..."
RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}" \
  --output json)

TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
echo "  Registration Token: $TOKEN"

# Step 4: Wait and verify
echo ""
echo "Step 4: Verifying registration..."
sleep 30
aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
  --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
  --output json | jq '{
    Status: .ProgressStatus,
    TypeArn: .TypeVersionArn,
    Description: .Description
  }'

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension registration workflow complete"
anihpj Extension Registration Workflow ======================================== Step 1: Building extension package... ✓ Package built: anihpj-django-application.zip Step 2: Uploading to S3... ✓ Uploaded to s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application-v3.0.zip Step 3: Registering type... Registration Token: reg-token-abc123-def456 Step 4: Verifying registration... { "Status": "COMPLETE", "TypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000003", "Description": "Registered anihpj Django application resource" } anihpj extension registration workflow complete
Example 5: Register All anihpj Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Batch register all anihpj extensions
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extensions"

declare -A EXTENSIONS=(
  ["anihpj::Django::Application"]="anihpj-django-application.zip"
  ["anihpj::Django::Database"]="anihpj-django-database.zip"
  ["anihpj::Django::Cache"]="anihpj-django-cache.zip"
)

echo "Registering anihpj Extension Suite"
echo "===================================="

for TYPE_NAME in "${!EXTENSIONS[@]}"; do
  PACKAGE="${EXTENSIONS[$TYPE_NAME]}"
  
  echo ""
  echo "Registering: $TYPE_NAME"
  
  RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
    --schema-handler-package "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${PACKAGE}" \
    --output json 2>&1)
  
  if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "RegistrationToken"; then
    TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
    echo "  Token: $TOKEN"
    echo "  Status: Pending"
  else
    echo "  Error: $RESULT"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "All anihpj extensions submitted for registration"
echo "Use list-type-registrations to monitor progress"
Registering anihpj Extension Suite ==================================== Registering: anihpj::Django::Application Token: reg-token-app-123 Status: Pending Registering: anihpj::Django::Database Token: reg-token-db-456 Status: Pending Registering: anihpj::Django::Cache Token: reg-token-cache-789 Status: Pending All anihpj extensions submitted for registration Use list-type-registrations to monitor progress

rollback-stack

Rolls back a stack in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state to the last successful state.

Syntax
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Rollback Failed anihpj Stack Update
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback anihpj stack stuck in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production

echo "anihpj stack rollback initiated"
anihpj stack rollback initiated Stack will return to last stable state
Example 2: Rollback anihpj Stack with Skip Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback while skipping problematic resources
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

# First identify resources causing issues
echo "Checking failed resources..."
FAILED_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackResources[?ResourceStatus==`UPDATE_FAILED` || ResourceStatus==`DELETE_FAILED`].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text)

echo "Failed resources: $FAILED_RESOURCES"
echo ""

# Rollback skipping those resources
echo "Rolling back anihpj stack..."
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --resources-to-skip $FAILED_RESOURCES

echo ""
echo "anihpj stack rollback started (skipping: $FAILED_RESOURCES)"
Checking failed resources... Failed resources: CustomLambdaFunction FailedSecurityGroup Rolling back anihpj stack... anihpj stack rollback started (skipping: CustomLambdaFunction FailedSecurityGroup)
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Stack Rollback
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback and monitor progress
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "Starting anihpj stack rollback..."
aws cloudformation rollback-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

echo ""
echo "Monitoring rollback progress..."
echo "==============================="

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  TIMESTAMP=$(date +%H:%M:%S)
  echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [[ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "✓ anihpj stack rollback completed successfully"
    break
  elif [[ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "✗ Rollback failed again"
    echo "Check stack events for details"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 15
done
Starting anihpj stack rollback... Monitoring rollback progress... =============================== [14:30:15] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:30:30] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:30:45] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:31:00] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE ✓ anihpj stack rollback completed successfully
Example 4: anihpj Stack Rollback with Reason Analysis
#!/bin/bash
# Analyze failure and perform rollback
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "anihpj Stack Failure Analysis & Rollback"
echo "=========================================="

# Get current status
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text)

echo "Current Status: $STATUS"
echo ""

if [ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]; then
  # Get failure reasons
  echo "Failure Reasons:"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceStatus==`UPDATE_FAILED`].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
    --output table
  
  echo ""
  read -p "Proceed with rollback? (y/n): " CONFIRM
  
  if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
    aws cloudformation rollback-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME
    echo "Rollback initiated for anihpj stack"
  fi
else
  echo "Stack is not in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state"
  echo "Current status: $STATUS"
fi
anihpj Stack Failure Analysis & Rollback ========================================== Current Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED Failure Reasons: +------------------------+------------------------------------------+ | CustomLambdaFunction | Resource update cancelled | | ApplicationDatabase | Cannot modify master password | +------------------------+------------------------------------------+ Proceed with rollback? (y/n): y Rollback initiated for anihpj stack
Example 5: Automated anihpj Rollback Script
#!/bin/bash
# Automated rollback script for anihpj stacks
rollback_stack() {
  local STACK_NAME=$1
  local MAX_RETRIES=${2:-3}
  local RETRY=0
  
  echo "anihpj Automated Rollback: $STACK_NAME"
  echo "========================================"
  
  while [ $RETRY -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
    STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ "$STATUS" != "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]; then
      echo "Stack not in rollback-failed state: $STATUS"
      return 0
    fi
    
    echo "Attempt $((RETRY + 1)) of $MAX_RETRIES"
    
    # Get resources to skip
    SKIP_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'StackResources[?ResourceStatus==`DELETE_FAILED`].LogicalResourceId' \
      --output text)
    
    if [ -n "$SKIP_RESOURCES" ]; then
      echo "Skipping resources: $SKIP_RESOURCES"
      aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
        --resources-to-skip $SKIP_RESOURCES
    else
      aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME
    fi
    
    # Wait for completion
    aws cloudformation wait stack-rollback-complete \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME 2>/dev/null
    
    NEW_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
      --output text)
    
    if [ "$NEW_STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE" ]; then
      echo "✓ Rollback successful"
      return 0
    fi
    
    RETRY=$((RETRY + 1))
  done
  
  echo "✗ Rollback failed after $MAX_RETRIES attempts"
  return 1
}

# Run rollback
rollback_stack "anihpj-django-production" 3
anihpj Automated Rollback: anihpj-django-production ======================================== Attempt 1 of 3 Skipping resources: FailedLambdaFunction ✓ Rollback successful anihpj stack restored to stable state

set-stack-policy

Sets a stack policy for a stack to prevent updates to protected resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --stack-policy-body file://{policy-file}
Django/anihpj Examples
Example 1: Protect anihpj Database from Updates
#!/bin/bash
# Create policy to protect database
cat > /tmp/anihpj-stack-policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": "Update:Replace",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjDatabase"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --stack-policy-body file:///tmp/anihpj-stack-policy.json

echo "anihpj database protected from replacement"
anihpj database protected from replacement Database cannot be replaced during stack updates
Example 2: Protect All anihpj Critical Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive protection policy for anihpj
cat > /tmp/anihpj-critical-policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": ["Update:Replace", "Update:Delete"],
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": [
        "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjDatabase",
        "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjElastiCache",
        "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjEFSFileSystem"
      ],
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {
          "ResourceType": [
            "AWS::RDS::DBInstance",
            "AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster",
            "AWS::EFS::FileSystem"
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:Modify",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*",
      "Condition": {
        "StringNotLike": {
          "ResourceType": [
            "AWS::RDS::*",
            "AWS::ElastiCache::*",
            "AWS::EFS::*"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --stack-policy-body file:///tmp/anihpj-critical-policy.json

echo "anihpj critical resources protected"
anihpj critical resources protected Protected resources: - AnihpjDatabase (RDS) - AnihpjElastiCache (ElastiCache) - AnihpjEFSFileSystem (EFS) These cannot be replaced or deleted during updates
Example 3: Lock Down anihpj Production Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Deny all updates except modify
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

cat > /tmp/anihpj-lockdown-policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": ["Update:Replace", "Update:Delete"],
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:Modify",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

echo "Locking down anihpj production stack..."

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-policy-body file:///tmp/anihpj-lockdown-policy.json

echo ""
echo "anihpj production stack locked down"
echo "Only in-place modifications allowed"
echo "No resource replacements or deletions permitted"
Locking down anihpj production stack... anihpj production stack locked down Only in-place modifications allowed No resource replacements or deletions permitted
Example 4: View and Update anihpj Stack Policy
#!/bin/bash
# View current policy and update
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "Current anihpj Stack Policy:"
echo "============================="
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' \
  --output text | jq '.'

echo ""
echo "Updating policy to add ALB protection..."

# Get existing policy and add new protection
EXISTING=$(aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' \
  --output text)

# Add ALB protection statement
UPDATED=$(echo "$EXISTING" | jq '.Statement += [{
  "Effect": "Deny",
  "Action": ["Update:Replace", "Update:Delete"],
  "Principal": "*",
  "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjLoadBalancer"
}]')

echo "$UPDATED" > /tmp/anihpj-updated-policy.json

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-policy-body file:///tmp/anihpj-updated-policy.json

echo "anihpj stack policy updated - ALB now protected"
Current anihpj Stack Policy: ============================= { "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Deny", "Action": "Update:Replace", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjDatabase" } ] } Updating policy to add ALB protection... anihpj stack policy updated - ALB now protected
Example 5: anihpj Environment-Based Stack Policies
#!/bin/bash
# Apply appropriate policies per environment
apply_stack_policy() {
  local STACK_NAME=$1
  local ENVIRONMENT=$2
  
  echo "Applying $ENVIRONMENT policy to $STACK_NAME"
  
  case $ENVIRONMENT in
    production)
      # Strict policy - protect everything
      cat > /tmp/policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": ["Update:Replace", "Update:Delete"],
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:Modify",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF
      ;;
    staging)
      # Moderate policy - protect data resources
      cat > /tmp/policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": "Update:Replace",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/AnihpjDatabase"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF
      ;;
    development)
      # Permissive policy - allow all
      cat > /tmp/policy.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF
      ;;
  esac
  
  aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --stack-policy-body file:///tmp/policy.json
  
  echo "  ✓ $ENVIRONMENT policy applied"
}

# Apply policies to all anihpj stacks
apply_stack_policy "anihpj-django-production" "production"
apply_stack_policy "anihpj-django-staging" "staging"
apply_stack_policy "anihpj-django-dev" "development"

echo ""
echo "anihpj stack policies configured by environment"
Applying production policy to anihpj-django-production ✓ production policy applied Applying staging policy to anihpj-django-staging ✓ staging policy applied Applying development policy to anihpj-django-dev ✓ development policy applied anihpj stack policies configured by environment

🐳 Containers

AWS Container services provide fully managed container orchestration and registry. This section covers ECS for container orchestration, EKS for Kubernetes, and ECR for container registry. Learn how to deploy your anihpj Django application in containers.
